verses.greek: τέξεται δὲ υἱόν καὶ καλέσεις τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ Ἰησοῦν αὐτὸς γὰρ σώσει τὸν λαὸν αὐτοῦ ἀπὸ τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν αὐτῶν
verses.transliteration: texetai de uion kai kaleseis to onoma autou Iēsoun autos gar sōsei ton laon autou apo tōn hamartiōn autōn
verses.referenceTranslation: She shall give birth to a son, and you shall call his name Jesus, for it is he who shall save his people from their sins.
verses.notes: Future indicatives τέξεται, σώσει. Name Ἰησοῦν explained etymologically.

verses.greek: καὶ λέγων μετανοεῖτε ἤγγικεν γὰρ ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν
verses.transliteration: kai legōn metanoeite ēngiken gar hē basileia tōn ouranōn
verses.referenceTranslation: And saying, "Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has drawn near."
verses.notes: Present imperative μετανοεῖτε — continuous call. Perfect ἤγγικεν — the kingdom has arrived and remains near.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν γέγραπται οὐκ ἐπ᾽ ἄρτῳ μόνῳ ζήσεται ὁ ἄνθρωπος ἀλλ᾽ ἐπὶ παντὶ ῥήματι ἐκπορευομένῳ διὰ στόματος θεοῦ
verses.transliteration: ho de apokritheis eipen gegraptai ouk ep artō monō zēsetai ho anthrōpos all epi panti rēmati ekporeuomenō dia stomatos theou
verses.referenceTranslation: But he answered, “It is written, ‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceeds out of the mouth of God.’”
verses.notes: Perfect passive γέγραπται introduces OT quotation (Deut 8:3). Dative ἐπ᾽ ἄρτῳ.

verses.greek: Μακάριοι οἱ πτωχοὶ τῷ πνεύματι, ὅτι αὐτῶν ἐστιν ἡ βασιλεία τῶν οὐρανῶν.
verses.transliteration: Makarioi hoi ptōchoi tō pneumati, hoti autōn estin hē basileia tōn ouranōn.
verses.referenceTranslation: Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
verses.notes: First Beatitude. Dative of reference τῷ πνεύματι.

verses.greek: μακάριοι οἱ καθαροὶ τῇ καρδίᾳ ὅτι αὐτοὶ τὸν θεὸν ὄψονται
verses.transliteration: makarioi oi katharoi tē kardia hoti autoi ton theon opsontai
verses.referenceTranslation: Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God.
verses.notes: Sixth Beatitude. Dative of reference τῇ καρδίᾳ. Future deponent ὄψονται.

verses.greek: μακάριοι οἱ εἰρηνοποιοί ὅτι αὐτοὶ υἱοὶ θεοῦ κληθήσονται
verses.transliteration: makarioi oi eirēnopoioi hoti autoi uioi theou klēthēsontai
verses.referenceTranslation: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called sons of God.
verses.notes: Seventh Beatitude. Compound noun εἰρηνοποιοί. Future passive κληθήσονται.

verses.greek: Ὑμεῖς ἐστε τὸ φῶς τοῦ κόσμου οὐ δύναται πόλις κρυβῆναι ἐπάνω ὄρους κειμένη
verses.transliteration: Hymeis este to phōs tou kosmou ou dynatai polis krybēnai epanō orous keimenē
verses.referenceTranslation: You are the light of the world. A city located on a hill can’t be hidden.
verses.notes: Predicate nominative τὸ φῶς. Aorist passive infinitive κρυβῆναι.

verses.greek: οὕτως λαμψάτω τὸ φῶς ὑμῶν ἔμπροσθεν τῶν ἀνθρώπων ὅπως ἴδωσιν ὑμῶν τὰ καλὰ ἔργα καὶ δοξάσωσιν τὸν πατέρα ὑμῶν τὸν ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς
verses.transliteration: outōs lampsatō to phōs hymōn emprosthen tōn anthrōpōn hopōs idōsin hymōn ta kala erga kai doxasōsin ton patera hymōn ton en tois ouranois
verses.referenceTranslation: In the same way, let your light shine before men, so that they may see your good works and glorify your Father who is in heaven.
verses.notes: Aorist imperative λαμψάτω. Purpose clause with ὅπως + subjunctive.

verses.greek: ἐγὼ δὲ λέγω ὑμῖν ἀγαπᾶτε τοὺς ἐχθροὺς ὑμῶν καὶ προσεύχεσθε ὑπὲρ τῶν διωκόντων ὑμᾶς
verses.transliteration: egō de legō hymin agapate tous echthrous hymōn kai proseuchesthe hyper tōn diōkontōn hymas
verses.referenceTranslation: But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,
verses.notes: Present imperatives ἀγαπᾶτε, προσεύχεσθε — continuous action. Substantival participle τῶν διωκόντων.

verses.greek: Οὕτως οὖν προσεύχεσθε ὑμεῖς· Πάτερ ἡμῶν ὁ ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς, ἁγιασθήτω τὸ ὄνομά σου·
verses.transliteration: Houtōs oun proseuchesthe hymeis: Pater hēmōn ho en tois ouranois, hagiasthētō to onoma sou;
verses.referenceTranslation: Pray then like this: 'Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name.'
verses.notes: Beginning of the Lord's Prayer. Aorist passive imperative ἁγιασθήτω.

verses.greek: ἐλθέτω ἡ βασιλεία σου γενηθήτω τὸ θέλημά σου ὡς ἐν οὐρανῷ καὶ ἐπὶ γῆς
verses.transliteration: elthetō hē basileia sou genēthētō to thelēma sou hōs en ouranō kai epi gēs
verses.referenceTranslation: Your kingdom come, your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven.
verses.notes: Two aorist passive imperatives ἐλθέτω, γενηθήτω. Comparative ὡς...καί construction.

verses.greek: τὸν ἄρτον ἡμῶν τὸν ἐπιούσιον δὸς ἡμῖν σήμερον
verses.transliteration: ton arton hēmōn ton epiousion dos hēmin sēmeron
verses.referenceTranslation: Give us today our daily bread.
verses.notes: Aorist imperative δός. Rare adjective ἐπιούσιον — meaning debated (daily/necessary).

verses.greek: καὶ ἄφες ἡμῖν τὰ ὀφειλήματα ἡμῶν ὡς καὶ ἡμεῖς ἀφήκαμεν τοῖς ὀφειλέταις ἡμῶν
verses.transliteration: kai aphes hēmin ta opheilēmata hēmōn hōs kai hēmeis aphēkamen tois opheiletais hēmōn
verses.referenceTranslation: And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors.
verses.notes: Aorist imperative ἄφες. Aorist ἀφήκαμεν — completed action of forgiveness.

verses.greek: καὶ μὴ εἰσενέγκῃς ἡμᾶς εἰς πειρασμόν ἀλλὰ ῥῦσαι ἡμᾶς ἀπὸ τοῦ πονηροῦ
verses.transliteration: kai mē eisenenkēs hēmas eis peirasmon alla rysai hēmas apo tou ponērou
verses.referenceTranslation: And do not lead us into temptation, but deliver us from evil.
verses.notes: Aorist subjunctive εἰσενέγκῃς with μή — prohibition. Aorist imperative ῥῦσαι.

verses.greek: ζητεῖτε δὲ πρῶτον τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ τὴν δικαιοσύνην αὐτοῦ καὶ ταῦτα πάντα προστεθήσεται ὑμῖν
verses.transliteration: zēteite de prōton tēn basileian tou theou kai tēn dikaiosynēn autou kai tauta panta prostethēsetai hymin
verses.referenceTranslation: But seek first God’s Kingdom, and his righteousness; and all these things will be given to you as well.
verses.notes: Present imperative ζητεῖτε — continuous seeking. Future passive προστεθήσεται.

verses.greek: Αἰτεῖτε καὶ δοθήσεται ὑμῖν ζητεῖτε καὶ εὑρήσετε κρούετε καὶ ἀνοιγήσεται ὑμῖν
verses.transliteration: Aiteite kai dothēsetai hymin zēteite kai eurēsete krouete kai anoigēsetai hymin
verses.referenceTranslation: Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and it will be opened unto you.
verses.notes: Three present imperatives (αἰτεῖτε, ζητεῖτε, κρούετε) paired with future passives. Continuous action.

verses.greek: Πάντα οὖν ὅσα ἐὰν θέλητε ἵνα ποιῶσιν ὑμῖν οἱ ἄνθρωποι οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς ποιεῖτε αὐτοῖς οὗτος γάρ ἐστιν ὁ νόμος καὶ οἱ προφῆται
verses.transliteration: Panta oun hosa ean thelēte hina poiōsin hymin oi anthrōpoi outōs kai hymeis poieite autois outos gar estin ho nomos kai oi prophētai
verses.referenceTranslation: Therefore whatever you desire that men should do to you, so also do to them, for this is the law and the prophets.
verses.notes: Golden Rule. ὅσα ἐάν + subjunctive — indefinite relative clause. Present imperative ποιεῖτε.

verses.greek: Δεῦτε πρός με πάντες οἱ κοπιῶντες καὶ πεφορτισμένοι, κἀγὼ ἀναπαύσω ὑμᾶς.
verses.transliteration: Deute pros me pantes hoi kopiōntes kai pephortismenoi, kagō anapausō humas.
verses.referenceTranslation: Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
verses.notes: Jesus' invitation. Substantival participles.

verses.greek: ἄρατε τὸν ζυγόν μου ἐφ᾽ ὑμᾶς καὶ μάθετε ἀπ᾽ ἐμοῦ ὅτι πραΰς εἰμι καὶ ταπεινὸς τῇ καρδίᾳ καὶ εὑρήσετε ἀνάπαυσιν ταῖς ψυχαῖς ὑμῶν
verses.transliteration: arate ton zygon mou eph hymas kai mathete ap emou hoti praus eimi kai tapeinos tē kardia kai eurēsete anapausin tais psychais hymōn
verses.referenceTranslation: Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart; and you will find rest for your souls.
verses.notes: Aorist imperatives ἄρατε, μάθετε. Predicate adjectives πραΰς, ταπεινός.

verses.greek: κἀγὼ δέ σοι λέγω ὅτι σὺ εἶ Πέτρος καὶ ἐπὶ ταύτῃ τῇ πέτρᾳ οἰκοδομήσω μου τὴν ἐκκλησίαν καὶ πύλαι ᾅδου οὐ κατισχύσουσιν αὐτῆς
verses.transliteration: kagō de soi legō hoti sy ei Petros kai epi tautē tē petra oikodomēsō mou tēn ekklēsian kai pylai adou ou katischysousin autēs
verses.referenceTranslation: And I also say to you that you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of Hades will not prevail against it.
verses.notes: Wordplay Πέτρος/πέτρα. Future active οἰκοδομήσω. Genitive τοῦ ᾅδου.

verses.greek: Τότε ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ εἴ τις θέλει ὀπίσω μου ἐλθεῖν ἀπαρνησάσθω ἑαυτὸν καὶ ἀράτω τὸν σταυρὸν αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀκολουθείτω μοι
verses.transliteration: Tote ho Iēsous eipen tois mathētais autou ei tis thelei opisō mou elthein aparnēsasthō heauton kai aratō ton stauron autou kai akoloutheitō moi
verses.referenceTranslation: Then Jesus said to his disciples, "If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me."
verses.notes: Third class condition εἴ τις θέλει. Aorist imperatives ἀπαρνησάσθω, ἀράτω. Present imperative ἀκολουθείτω — continuous following.

verses.greek: οὗ γάρ εἰσιν δύο ἢ τρεῖς συνηγμένοι εἰς τὸ ἐμὸν ὄνομα ἐκεῖ εἰμι ἐν μέσῳ αὐτῶν
verses.transliteration: ou gar eisin dyo ē treis synēgmenoi eis to emon onoma ekei eimi en mesō autōn
verses.referenceTranslation: "For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there I am in their midst."
verses.notes: Perfect passive participle συνηγμένοι. Periphrastic εἰμί ἐν μέσῳ.

verses.greek: ἀγαπήσεις κύριον τὸν θεόν σου ἐν ὅλῃ τῇ καρδίᾳ σου... ἀγαπήσεις τὸν πλησίον σου ὡς σεαυτόν.
verses.transliteration: Agapēseis kurion ton theon sou en holē tē kardia sou... agapēseis ton plēsion sou hōs seauton.
verses.referenceTranslation: Jesus said to him, “‘You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.’
verses.notes: Greatest commandment. Future indicative as imperative.

verses.greek: δευτέρα δὲ ὁμοία αὐτῇ ἀγαπήσεις τὸν πλησίον σου ὡς σεαυτόν
verses.transliteration: deutera de homoia autē agapēseis ton plēsion sou hōs seauton
verses.referenceTranslation: A second likewise is this, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’
verses.notes: Future indicative ἀγαπήσεις used as imperative (Semitism). Reflexive pronoun σεαυτόν.

verses.greek: καὶ προσελθὼν ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἐλάλησεν αὐτοῖς λέγων ἐδόθη μοι πᾶσα ἐξουσία ἐν οὐρανῷ καὶ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς
verses.transliteration: kai proselthōn ho Iēsous elalēsen autois legōn edothē moi pasa exousia en ouranō kai epi tēs gēs
verses.referenceTranslation: And Jesus came and spoke to them, saying, "All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me."
verses.notes: Aorist passive ἐδόθη — divine passive. Attributive πᾶσα ἐξουσία.

verses.greek: πορευθέντες οὖν μαθητεύσατε πάντα τὰ ἔθνη, βαπτίζοντες αὐτοὺς εἰς τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ πατρὸς καὶ τοῦ υἱοῦ καὶ τοῦ ἁγίου πνεύματος,
verses.transliteration: Poreuthentes oun mathēteusate panta ta ethnē, baptizontes autous eis to onoma tou patros kai tou huiou kai tou hagiou pneumatos,
verses.referenceTranslation: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
verses.notes: The Great Commission. Main verb is μαθητεύσατε (aorist imperative).

verses.greek: διδάσκοντες αὐτοὺς τηρεῖν πάντα ὅσα ἐνετειλάμην ὑμῖν καὶ ἰδοὺ ἐγὼ μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν εἰμι πάσας τὰς ἡμέρας ἕως τῆς συντελείας τοῦ αἰῶνος
verses.transliteration: didaskontes autous tērein panta hosa eneteilamēn hymin kai idou egō meth hymōn eimi pasas tas hēmeras heōs tēs synteleias tou aiōnos
verses.referenceTranslation: teaching them to observe all things that I commanded you. And behold, I am with you always, even to the end of the age.
verses.notes: Present participle διδάσκοντες — attendant circumstance. ἰδού + nominative for emphasis.

verses.greek: Ἀρχὴ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ υἱοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.transliteration: Archē tou euangeliou Iēsou Christou huiou theou.
verses.referenceTranslation: The beginning of the Good News of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
verses.notes: Opening of Mark. Chain of genitives.

verses.greek: καὶ λέγων ὅτι πεπλήρωται ὁ καιρὸς καὶ ἤγγικεν ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ· μετανοεῖτε καὶ πιστεύετε ἐν τῷ εὐαγγελίῳ.
verses.transliteration: Kai legōn hoti peplērōtai ho kairos kai ēngiken hē basileia tou theou; metanoeite kai pisteuete en tō euangeliō.
verses.referenceTranslation: And saying, 'The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand; repent and believe in the gospel.'
verses.notes: Jesus' inaugural message. Perfect tenses πεπλήρωται and ἤγγικεν.

verses.greek: Καὶ προσκαλεσάμενος τὸν ὄχλον σὺν τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς εἴ τις θέλει ὀπίσω μου ἀκολουθεῖν ἀπαρνησάσθω ἑαυτὸν καὶ ἀράτω τὸν σταυρὸν αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀκολουθείτω μοι
verses.transliteration: Kai proskalesamenos ton ochlon syn tois mathētais autou eipen autois ei tis thelei opisō mou akolouthein aparnēsasthō heauton kai aratō ton stauron autou kai akoloutheitō moi
verses.referenceTranslation: And having called the crowd together with his disciples, he said to them, "If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me."
verses.notes: Aorist participle προσκαλεσάμενος. Three imperatives: ἀπαρνησάσθω, ἀράτω, ἀκολουθείτω.

verses.greek: ὃς γὰρ ἐὰν θέλῃ τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ σῶσαι ἀπολέσει αὐτήν ὃς δ᾽ ἂν ἀπολέσει τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ ἕνεκεν ἐμοῦ καὶ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου σώσει αὐτήν
verses.transliteration: hos gar ean thelē tēn psychēn autou sōsai apolesei autēn hos d an apolesei tēn psychēn autou heneken emou kai tou euangeliou sōsei autēn
verses.referenceTranslation: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for my sake and the gospel's will save it.
verses.notes: Conditional with ἐάν + subjunctive. Paradox: σῶσαι/ἀπολέσει.

verses.greek: καὶ γὰρ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου οὐκ ἦλθεν διακονηθῆναι ἀλλὰ διακονῆσαι καὶ δοῦναι τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ λύτρον ἀντὶ πολλῶν
verses.transliteration: kai gar ho uios tou anthrōpou ouk ēlthen diakonēthēnai alla diakonēsai kai dounai tēn psychēn autou lytron anti pollōn
verses.referenceTranslation: For the Son of Man also came not to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
verses.notes: Purpose infinitives διακονηθῆναι, διακονῆσαι, δοῦναι. λύτρον ἀντί — ransom-price.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀγαπήσεις κύριον τὸν θεόν σου ἐξ ὅλης τῆς καρδίας σου καὶ ἐξ ὅλης τῆς ψυχῆς σου καὶ ἐξ ὅλης τῆς διανοίας σου καὶ ἐξ ὅλης τῆς ἰσχύος σου
verses.transliteration: kai agapēseis kyrion ton theon sou ex holēs tēs kardias sou kai ex holēs tēs psychēs sou kai ex holēs tēs dianoias sou kai ex holēs tēs ischyos sou
verses.referenceTranslation: And you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind, and with all your strength.
verses.notes: Future indicative ἀγαπήσεις as imperative. Shema quotation (Deut 6:5). Fourfold ἐξ ὅλης.

verses.greek: δευτέρα αὕτη ἀγαπήσεις τὸν πλησίον σου ὡς σεαυτόν μείζων τούτων ἄλλη ἐντολὴ οὐκ ἔστιν
verses.transliteration: deutera autē agapēseis ton plēsion sou hōs seauton meizōn toutōn allē entolē ouk estin
verses.referenceTranslation: The second is this: 'You shall love your neighbor as yourself.' There is no other commandment greater than these.
verses.notes: Comparative μείζων — no commandment greater than these two.

verses.greek: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς πορευθέντες εἰς τὸν κόσμον ἅπαντα κηρύξατε τὸ εὐαγγέλιον πάσῃ τῇ κτίσει
verses.transliteration: kai eipen autois poreuthentes eis ton kosmon hapanta kēryxate to euangelion pasē tē ktisei
verses.referenceTranslation: And he said to them, "Go into all the world and proclaim the gospel to all creation."
verses.notes: Aorist participle πορευθέντες + aorist imperative κηρύξατε. Universal scope: πάσῃ τῇ κτίσει.

verses.greek: ὁ πιστεύσας καὶ βαπτισθεὶς σωθήσεται ὁ δὲ ἀπιστήσας κατακριθήσεται
verses.transliteration: ho pisteusas kai baptistheis sōthēsetai ho de apistēsas katakrithēsetai
verses.referenceTranslation: The one who has believed and has been baptized will be saved, but the one who has disbelieved will be condemned.
verses.notes: Aorist participles πιστεύσας, βαπτισθείς. Future passives σωθήσεται, κατακριθήσεται.

verses.greek: Ἐπειδήπερ πολλοὶ ἐπεχείρησαν ἀνατάξασθαι διήγησιν περὶ τῶν πεπληροφορημένων ἐν ἡμῖν πραγμάτων.
verses.transliteration: Epeidēper polloi epecheirēsan anataxasthai diēgēsin peri tōn peplērophorēmenōn en hēmin pragmatōn.
verses.referenceTranslation: Inasmuch as many have undertaken to compile a narrative of the things that have been accomplished among us.
verses.notes: Classical Greek prologue. Complex sentence structure with perfect passive participle.

verses.greek: ὅτι οὐκ ἀδυνατήσει παρὰ τοῦ θεοῦ πᾶν ῥῆμα
verses.transliteration: hoti ouk adynatēsei para tou theou pan rēma
verses.referenceTranslation: For nothing will be impossible with God.
verses.notes: Future active ἀδυνατήσει. παρὰ τοῦ θεοῦ — nothing is impossible 'with' God.

verses.greek: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἄγγελος· μὴ φοβεῖσθε· ἰδοὺ γὰρ εὐαγγελίζομαι ὑμῖν χαρὰν μεγάλην ἥτις ἔσται παντὶ τῷ λαῷ, ὅτι ἐτέχθη ὑμῖν σήμερον σωτὴρ ὅς ἐστιν Χριστὸς κύριος ἐν πόλει Δαυίδ.
verses.transliteration: Kai eipen autois ho angelos: Mē phobeisthe; idou gar euangelizomai hymin charan megalēn hētis estai panti tō laō, hoti etechthē hymin sēmeron sōtēr hos estin Christos kyrios en polei Dauid.
verses.referenceTranslation: The angel said to them, “Don’t be afraid, for behold, I bring you good news of great joy which will be to all the people.
verses.notes: Christmas narrative. εὐαγγελίζομαι is cognate with εὐαγγέλιον.

verses.greek: Μὴ φοβεῖσθε· ἰδοὺ γὰρ εὐαγγελίζομαι ὑμῖν χαρὰν μεγάλην ἥτις ἔσται παντὶ τῷ λαῷ.
verses.transliteration: Mē phobeisthe; idou gar euangelizomai humin charan megalēn hētis estai panti tō laō.
verses.referenceTranslation: Do not be afraid. I bring you good news of great joy that will be for all the people.
verses.notes: Angel's announcement. Present imperative with μή for prohibition.

verses.greek: ὅτι ἐτέχθη ὑμῖν σήμερον σωτὴρ ὅς ἐστιν χριστὸς κύριος ἐν πόλει Δαυίδ
verses.transliteration: hoti etechthē hymin sēmeron sōtēr hos estin christos kyrios en polei Dauid
verses.referenceTranslation: For there is born to you today, in David’s city, a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.
verses.notes: Aorist passive ἐτέχθη. Triple title: σωτήρ, χριστός, κύριος.

verses.greek: δόξα ἐν ὑψίστοις θεῷ καὶ ἐπὶ γῆς εἰρήνη ἐν ἀνθρώποις εὐδοκίας
verses.transliteration: doxa en hypsistois theō kai epi gēs eirēnē en anthrōpois eudokias
verses.referenceTranslation: “Glory in the highest, to God and on earth peace among men with whom He is pleased!”
verses.notes: Genitive εὐδοκίας modifies ἀνθρώποις — 'men of [God's] good pleasure.'

verses.greek: πνεῦμα κυρίου ἐπ᾽ ἐμὲ οὗ εἵνεκεν ἔχρισέν με εὐαγγελίσασθαι πτωχοῖς ἀπέσταλκέν με κηρύξαι αἰχμαλώτοις ἄφεσιν καὶ τυφλοῖς ἀνάβλεψιν ἀποστεῖλαι τεθραυσμένους ἐν ἀφέσει
verses.transliteration: pneuma kyriou ep eme ou eineken echrisen me euangelisasthai ptōchois apestalken me kēryxai aichmalōtois aphesin kai typhlois anablepsin aposteilai tethrausmenous en aphesei
verses.referenceTranslation: “The Spirit of the Lord is upon Me, of which because He has anointed Me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent Me to proclaim to the captives, deliverance and to the blind, recovery of sight to send forth the oppressed, in deliverance
verses.notes: Isaiah 61:1 quotation. Aorist ἔχρισέν, perfect ἀπέσταλκέν.

verses.greek: Καὶ καθὼς θέλετε ἵνα ποιῶσιν ὑμῖν οἱ ἄνθρωποι ποιεῖτε αὐτοῖς ὁμοίως
verses.transliteration: Kai kathōs thelete hina poiōsin hymin oi anthrōpoi poieite autois homoiōs
verses.referenceTranslation: And as you desire that men should do to you, do likewise to them.
verses.notes: Golden Rule (Lukan form). Present subjunctive ποιῶσιν. ὁμοίως — 'in the same way.'

verses.greek: Ἔλεγεν δὲ πρὸς πάντας εἴ τις θέλει ὀπίσω μου ἔρχεσθαι ἀρνησάσθω ἑαυτὸν καὶ ἀράτω τὸν σταυρὸν αὐτοῦ καθ᾽ ἡμέραν καὶ ἀκολουθείτω μοι
verses.transliteration: Elegen de pros pantas ei tis thelei opisō mou erchesthai arnēsasthō heauton kai aratō ton stauron autou kath hēmeran kai akoloutheitō moi
verses.referenceTranslation: And he was saying to all, "If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross daily, and follow me."
verses.notes: Imperfect ἔλεγεν — habitual teaching. Luke adds καθ᾽ ἡμέραν (daily). Three imperatives.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν ἀγαπήσεις κύριον τὸν θεόν σου ἐξ ὅλης τῆς καρδίας σου καὶ ἐν ὅλῃ τῇ ψυχῇ σου καὶ ἐν ὅλῃ τῇ ἰσχύϊ σου καὶ ἐν ὅλῃ τῇ διανοίᾳ σου καὶ τὸν πλησίον σου ὡς σεαυτόν
verses.transliteration: ho de apokritheis eipen agapēseis kyrion ton theon sou ex holēs tēs kardias sou kai en holē tē psychē sou kai en holē tē ischui sou kai en holē tē dianoia sou kai ton plēsion sou hōs seauton
verses.referenceTranslation: And he answered, 'You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your strength, and with all your mind; and your neighbor as yourself.'
verses.notes: Combines Deut 6:5 and Lev 19:18. Aorist participle ἀποκριθείς.

verses.greek: Κἀγὼ ὑμῖν λέγω αἰτεῖτε καὶ δοθήσεται ὑμῖν ζητεῖτε καὶ εὑρήσετε κρούετε καὶ ἀνοιγήσεται ὑμῖν
verses.transliteration: Kagō hymin legō aiteite kai dothēsetai hymin zēteite kai eurēsete krouete kai anoigēsetai hymin
verses.referenceTranslation: And I say to you: Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and it will be opened to you.
verses.notes: Three present imperatives matched with future passives. κἀγώ = καὶ ἐγώ (crasis).

verses.greek: λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι οὕτως χαρὰ ἐν τῷ οὐρανῷ ἔσται ἐπὶ ἑνὶ ἁμαρτωλῷ μετανοοῦντι ἢ ἐπὶ ἐνενήκοντα ἐννέα δικαίοις οἵτινες οὐ χρείαν ἔχουσιν μετανοίας
verses.transliteration: legō hymin hoti outōs chara en tō ouranō estai epi heni hamartōlō metanoounti ē epi enenēkonta ennea dikaiois oitines ou chreian echousin metanoias
verses.referenceTranslation: I say to you that in the same way joy in heaven there will be over one sinner repenting, rather than over ninety nine righteous ones who no need have of repentance.
verses.notes: Future ἔσται. Present participle μετανοοῦντι — a sinner who is repenting.

verses.greek: ἦλθεν γὰρ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ζητῆσαι καὶ σῶσαι τὸ ἀπολωλός
verses.transliteration: ēlthen gar ho uios tou anthrōpou zētēsai kai sōsai to apolōlos
verses.referenceTranslation: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save that which was lost.”
verses.notes: Purpose infinitives ζητῆσαι, σῶσαι. Perfect participle τὸ ἀπολωλός — 'the one having been lost.'

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς ἔλεγεν πάτερ ἄφες αὐτοῖς οὐ γὰρ οἴδασιν τί ποιοῦσιν διαμεριζόμενοι δὲ τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτοῦ ἔβαλον κλήρους
verses.transliteration: ho de Iēsous elegen pater aphes autois ou gar oidasin ti poiousin diamerizomenoi de ta himatia autou ebalon klērous
verses.referenceTranslation: Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they don’t know what they are doing.” Dividing his garments among them, they cast lots.
verses.notes: Present imperative ἄφες. Causal γάρ — reason for forgiveness request.

verses.greek: οὐκ ἔστιν ὧδε ἀλλ᾽ ἠγέρθη μνήσθητε ὡς ἐλάλησεν ὑμῖν ἔτι ὢν ἐν τῇ Γαλιλαίᾳ
verses.transliteration: ouk estin hōde all ēgerthē mnēsthēte hōs elalēsen hymin eti ōn en tē Galilaia
verses.referenceTranslation: He isn’t here, but is risen. Remember what he told you when he was still in Galilee,
verses.notes: Aorist passive ἠγέρθη — divine passive. Aorist imperative μνήσθητε.

verses.greek: λέγων τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ὅτι δεῖ παραδοθῆναι εἰς χεῖρας ἀνθρώπων ἁμαρτωλῶν καὶ σταυρωθῆναι καὶ τῇ τρίτῃ ἡμέρᾳ ἀναστῆναι
verses.transliteration: legōn ton uion tou anthrōpou hoti dei paradothēnai eis cheiras anthrōpōn hamartōlōn kai staurōthēnai kai tē tritē hēmera anastēnai
verses.referenceTranslation: saying that the Son of Man must be delivered up into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again?”
verses.notes: Infinitives παραδοθῆναι, σταυρωθῆναι, ἀναστῆναι. δεῖ — divine necessity.

verses.greek: Ἐν ἀρχῇ ἦν ὁ λόγος, καὶ ὁ λόγος ἦν πρὸς τὸν θεόν, καὶ θεὸς ἦν ὁ λόγος.
verses.transliteration: En archē ēn ho logos, kai ho logos ēn pros ton theon, kai theos ēn ho logos.
verses.referenceTranslation: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
verses.notes: Famous prologue of John's Gospel. Note the articular vs anarthrous θεός.

verses.greek: ἐν αὐτῷ ζωὴ ἦν καὶ ἡ ζωὴ ἦν τὸ φῶς τῶν ἀνθρώπων
verses.transliteration: en autō zōē ēn kai hē zōē ēn to phōs tōn anthrōpōn
verses.referenceTranslation: In him was life, and the life was the light of men.
verses.notes: Imperfect ἦν — continuous past existence. ζωή and φῶς as theological concepts.

verses.greek: καὶ τὸ φῶς ἐν τῇ σκοτίᾳ φαίνει καὶ ἡ σκοτία αὐτὸ οὐ κατέλαβεν
verses.transliteration: kai to phōs en tē skotia phainei kai hē skotia auto ou katelaben
verses.referenceTranslation: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness hasn’t overcome it.
verses.notes: Present φαίνει — the light keeps shining. Aorist κατέλαβεν — ambiguous: 'comprehend' or 'overcome.'

verses.greek: Ἦν τὸ φῶς τὸ ἀληθινόν ὃ φωτίζει πάντα ἄνθρωπον ἐρχόμενον εἰς τὸν κόσμον
verses.transliteration: Ēn to phōs to alēthinon ho phōtizei panta anthrōpon erchomenon eis ton kosmon
verses.referenceTranslation: The true light, which enlightens every man, was coming into the world.
verses.notes: Periphrastic imperfect Ἦν...ἐρχόμενον. ἀληθινόν — 'genuine/real' light.

verses.greek: ἐν τῷ κόσμῳ ἦν καὶ ὁ κόσμος δι᾽ αὐτοῦ ἐγένετο καὶ ὁ κόσμος αὐτὸν οὐκ ἔγνω
verses.transliteration: en tō kosmō ēn kai ho kosmos di autou egeneto kai ho kosmos auton ouk egnō
verses.referenceTranslation: He was in the world, and the world was made through him, and the world did not know him.
verses.notes: Three uses of κόσμος. Aorist ἐγένετο — came into being. Aorist ἔγνω — the world did not know.

verses.greek: εἰς τὰ ἴδια ἦλθεν καὶ οἱ ἴδιοι αὐτὸν οὐ παρέλαβον
verses.transliteration: eis ta idia ēlthen kai oi idioi auton ou parelabon
verses.referenceTranslation: He came to his own, and his own people did not receive him.
verses.notes: τὰ ἴδια (neuter) = his own things/place; οἱ ἴδιοι (masculine) = his own people. Aorist παρέλαβον.

verses.greek: ὅσοι δὲ ἔλαβον αὐτόν ἔδωκεν αὐτοῖς ἐξουσίαν τέκνα θεοῦ γενέσθαι τοῖς πιστεύουσιν εἰς τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ
verses.transliteration: hosoi de elabon auton edōken autois exousian tekna theou genesthai tois pisteuousin eis to onoma autou
verses.referenceTranslation: But as many as received him, to them he gave the right to become God’s children, to those who believe in his name:
verses.notes: Aorist ἔλαβον, ἔδωκεν. ἐξουσία — authority/right. Epexegetical infinitive γενέσθαι.

verses.greek: Καὶ ὁ λόγος σὰρξ ἐγένετο καὶ ἐσκήνωσεν ἐν ἡμῖν, καὶ ἐθεασάμεθα τὴν δόξαν αὐτοῦ, δόξαν ὡς μονογενοῦς παρὰ πατρός, πλήρης χάριτος καὶ ἀληθείας.
verses.transliteration: Kai ho logos sarx egeneto kai eskēnōsen en hēmin, kai etheasametha tēn doxan autou, doxan hōs monogenous para patros, plērēs charitos kai alētheias.
verses.referenceTranslation: The Word became flesh, and lived among us. We saw his glory, such glory as of the one and only Son of the Father, full of grace and truth.
verses.notes: The incarnation. ἐσκήνωσεν literally 'tabernacled' among us.

verses.greek: ὅτι ἐκ τοῦ πληρώματος αὐτοῦ ἡμεῖς πάντες ἐλάβομεν καὶ χάριν ἀντὶ χάριτος
verses.transliteration: hoti ek tou plērōmatos autou hēmeis pantes elabomen kai charin anti charitos
verses.referenceTranslation: From his fullness we all received grace upon grace.
verses.notes: ἐκ τοῦ πληρώματος — from the fullness. χάριν ἀντὶ χάριτος — grace replacing grace.

verses.greek: ὅτι ὁ νόμος διὰ Μωϋσέως ἐδόθη ἡ χάρις καὶ ἡ ἀλήθεια διὰ Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ ἐγένετο
verses.transliteration: hoti ho nomos dia Mōyseōs edothē hē charis kai hē alētheia dia Iēsou Christou egeneto
verses.referenceTranslation: For the law was given through Moses. Grace and truth were realized through Jesus Christ.
verses.notes: Contrast: νόμος through Moses vs. χάρις and ἀλήθεια through Jesus Christ.

verses.greek: Θεὸν οὐδεὶς ἑώρακεν πώποτε μονογενὴς θεὸς ὁ ὢν εἰς τὸν κόλπον τοῦ πατρὸς ἐκεῖνος ἐξηγήσατο
verses.transliteration: Theon oudeis heōraken pōpote monogenēs theos ho ōn eis ton kolpon tou patros ekeinos exēgēsato
verses.referenceTranslation: No one has seen God at any time. The one and only Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, he has declared him.
verses.notes: Perfect ἑώρακεν — no one has ever seen. Aorist ἐξηγήσατο — 'he has made known/exegeted.'

verses.greek: ἀπεκρίθη Ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω σοι ἐὰν μή τις γεννηθῇ ἄνωθεν οὐ δύναται ἰδεῖν τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.transliteration: apekrithē Iēsous kai eipen autō amēn amēn legō soi ean mē tis gennēthē anōthen ou dynatai idein tēn basileian tou theou
verses.referenceTranslation: Jesus answered and said to him, "Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born from above, he cannot see the kingdom of God."
verses.notes: Double ἀμήν — solemn affirmation unique to John. ἄνωθεν — ambiguous: 'from above' or 'again.'

verses.greek: ἀπεκρίθη Ἰησοῦς ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω σοι ἐὰν μή τις γεννηθῇ ἐξ ὕδατος καὶ πνεύματος οὐ δύναται εἰσελθεῖν εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.transliteration: apekrithē Iēsous amēn amēn legō soi ean mē tis gennēthē ex hydatos kai pneumatos ou dynatai eiselthein eis tēn basileian tou theou
verses.referenceTranslation: Jesus answered, "Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter the kingdom of God."
verses.notes: ἐξ ὕδατος καὶ πνεύματος — single preposition governs both nouns. Aorist subjunctive γεννηθῇ.

verses.greek: μὴ θαυμάσῃς ὅτι εἶπόν σοι δεῖ ὑμᾶς γεννηθῆναι ἄνωθεν
verses.transliteration: mē thaumasēs hoti eipon soi dei hymas gennēthēnai anōthen
verses.referenceTranslation: {Do} not wonder that I said to you, ‘It is necessary for you all to be born from above.’
verses.notes: Aorist subjunctive θαυμάσῃς with μή — prohibition. δεῖ — impersonal 'it is necessary.'

verses.greek: οὕτως γὰρ ἠγάπησεν ὁ θεὸς τὸν κόσμον, ὥστε τὸν υἱὸν τὸν μονογενῆ ἔδωκεν, ἵνα πᾶς ὁ πιστεύων εἰς αὐτὸν μὴ ἀπόληται ἀλλ᾽ ἔχῃ ζωὴν αἰώνιον.
verses.transliteration: Houtōs gar ēgapēsen ho theos ton kosmon, hōste ton huion ton monogenē edōken, hina pas ho pisteuōn eis auton mē apolētai all' echē zōēn aiōnion.
verses.referenceTranslation: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish but have eternal life.
verses.notes: One of the most well-known verses. Note the purpose clause with ἵνα.

verses.greek: οὐ γὰρ ἀπέστειλεν ὁ θεὸς τὸν υἱὸν εἰς τὸν κόσμον ἵνα κρίνῃ τὸν κόσμον ἀλλ᾽ ἵνα σωθῇ ὁ κόσμος δι᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.transliteration: ou gar apesteilen ho theos ton uion eis ton kosmon hina krinē ton kosmon all hina sōthē ho kosmos di autou
verses.referenceTranslation: For God didn’t send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world should be saved through him.
verses.notes: Purpose clauses with ἵνα + subjunctive: κρίνῃ (judge) vs. σωθῇ (be saved).

verses.greek: ὁ πιστεύων εἰς αὐτὸν οὐ κρίνεται ὁ δὲ μὴ πιστεύων ἤδη κέκριται ὅτι μὴ πεπίστευκεν εἰς τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ μονογενοῦς υἱοῦ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.transliteration: ho pisteuōn eis auton ou krinetai ho de mē pisteuōn ēdē kekritai hoti mē pepisteuken eis to onoma tou monogenous uiou tou theou
verses.referenceTranslation: He who believes in him is not judged. He who doesn’t believe has been judged already, because he has not believed in the name of the one and only Son of God.
verses.notes: Present participle ὁ πιστεύων — ongoing belief. Perfect κέκριται — permanent state.

verses.greek: αὕτη δέ ἐστιν ἡ κρίσις ὅτι τὸ φῶς ἐλήλυθεν εἰς τὸν κόσμον καὶ ἠγάπησαν οἱ ἄνθρωποι μᾶλλον τὸ σκότος ἢ τὸ φῶς ἦν γὰρ αὐτῶν πονηρὰ τὰ ἔργα
verses.transliteration: autē de estin hē krisis hoti to phōs elēlythen eis ton kosmon kai ēgapēsan oi anthrōpoi mallon to skotos ē to phōs ēn gar autōn ponēra ta erga
verses.referenceTranslation: And this is the judgment: the light has come into the world, and men loved the darkness rather than the light, for their deeds were evil.
verses.notes: Perfect ἐλήλυθεν — the light has come and remains. Aorist ἠγάπησαν — they loved.

verses.greek: ὁ πιστεύων εἰς τὸν υἱὸν ἔχει ζωὴν αἰώνιον ὁ δὲ ἀπειθῶν τῷ υἱῷ οὐκ ὄψεται ζωήν ἀλλ᾽ ἡ ὀργὴ τοῦ θεοῦ μένει ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν
verses.transliteration: ho pisteuōn eis ton uion echei zōēn aiōnion ho de apeithōn tō uiō ouk opsetai zōēn all hē orgē tou theou menei ep auton
verses.referenceTranslation: The one who believes in the Son has eternal life, but the one who disobeys the Son will not see life, but the wrath of God remains on him.
verses.notes: Present participles ὁ πιστεύων, ὁ ἀπειθῶν. Contrast: ἔχει (has) vs. οὐκ ὄψεται (will not see).

verses.greek: πνεῦμα ὁ θεός καὶ τοὺς προσκυνοῦντας αὐτὸν ἐν πνεύματι καὶ ἀληθείᾳ δεῖ προσκυνεῖν
verses.transliteration: pneuma ho theos kai tous proskynountas auton en pneumati kai alētheia dei proskynein
verses.referenceTranslation: God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth.”
verses.notes: Predicate nominative πνεῦμα ὁ θεός. δεῖ + infinitive — must worship.

verses.greek: Ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ὁ τὸν λόγον μου ἀκούων καὶ πιστεύων τῷ πέμψαντί με ἔχει ζωὴν αἰώνιον καὶ εἰς κρίσιν οὐκ ἔρχεται ἀλλὰ μεταβέβηκεν ἐκ τοῦ θανάτου εἰς τὴν ζωήν
verses.transliteration: Amēn amēn legō hymin hoti ho ton logon mou akouōn kai pisteuōn tō pempsanti me echei zōēn aiōnion kai eis krisin ouk erchetai alla metabebēken ek tou thanatou eis tēn zōēn
verses.referenceTranslation: "Truly, truly, I say to you, the one who hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life. He does not come into judgment, but has passed from death to life."
verses.notes: Double ἀμήν. Substantival participles ὁ ἀκούων, πιστεύων. Perfect μεταβέβηκεν — permanent transfer.

verses.greek: εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἐγώ εἰμι ὁ ἄρτος τῆς ζωῆς ὁ ἐρχόμενος πρὸς ἐμὲ οὐ μὴ πεινάσῃ καὶ ὁ πιστεύων εἰς ἐμὲ οὐ μὴ διψήσει πώποτε
verses.transliteration: eipen autois ho Iēsous egō eimi ho artos tēs zōēs ho erchomenos pros eme ou mē peinasē kai ho pisteuōn eis eme ou mē dipsēsei pōpote
verses.referenceTranslation: Jesus said to them, "I am the bread of life. The one who comes to me shall never hunger, and the one who believes in me shall never thirst."
verses.notes: First 'I am' statement with predicate. οὐ μή + subjunctive — emphatic negation.

verses.greek: πᾶν ὃ δίδωσίν μοι ὁ πατὴρ πρὸς ἐμὲ ἥξει καὶ τὸν ἐρχόμενον πρὸς ἐμὲ οὐ μὴ ἐκβάλω ἔξω
verses.transliteration: pan ho didōsin moi ho patēr pros eme hēxei kai ton erchomenon pros eme ou mē ekbalō exō
verses.referenceTranslation: All those whom the Father gives me will come to me. He who comes to me I will in no way throw out.
verses.notes: Neuter πᾶν ὅ — collective 'all that.' οὐ μὴ ἐκβάλω ἔξω — emphatic double negative.

verses.greek: τοῦτο γάρ ἐστιν τὸ θέλημα τοῦ πατρός μου ἵνα πᾶς ὁ θεωρῶν τὸν υἱὸν καὶ πιστεύων εἰς αὐτὸν ἔχῃ ζωὴν αἰώνιον καὶ ἀναστήσω αὐτὸν ἐγὼ ἐν τῇ ἐσχάτῃ ἡμέρᾳ
verses.transliteration: touto gar estin to thelēma tou patros mou hina pas ho theōrōn ton uion kai pisteuōn eis auton echē zōēn aiōnion kai anastēsō auton egō en tē eschatē hēmera
verses.referenceTranslation: This is the will of the one who sent me, that everyone who sees the Son, and believes in him, should have eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.”
verses.notes: Purpose clause ἵνα. Present participles ὁ θεωρῶν, πιστεύων. Future ἀναστήσω.

verses.greek: Ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὁ πιστεύων ἔχει ζωὴν αἰώνιον
verses.transliteration: Amēn amēn legō hymin ho pisteuōn echei zōēn aiōnion
verses.referenceTranslation: Truly, truly, I say to you, the one who believes has eternal life.
verses.notes: Double ἀμήν. Present participle ὁ πιστεύων — ongoing faith.

verses.greek: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτῷ Σίμων Πέτρος κύριε πρὸς τίνα ἀπελευσόμεθα ῥήματα ζωῆς αἰωνίου ἔχεις
verses.transliteration: apekrithē autō Simōn Petros kyrie pros tina apeleusometha rēmata zōēs aiōniou echeis
verses.referenceTranslation: Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
verses.notes: Deliberative future ἀπελευσόμεθα — 'where shall we go?' ῥήματα ζωῆς αἰωνίου — words of eternal life.

verses.greek: Ἐν δὲ τῇ ἐσχάτῃ ἡμέρᾳ τῇ μεγάλῃ τῆς ἑορτῆς εἱστήκει ὁ Ἰησοῦς καὶ ἔκραξεν λέγων ἐάν τις διψᾷ ἐρχέσθω πρός με καὶ πινέτω
verses.transliteration: En de tē eschatē hēmera tē megalē tēs heortēs eistēkei ho Iēsous kai ekraxen legōn ean tis dipsa erchesthō pros me kai pinetō
verses.referenceTranslation: On the last day, the great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried out, saying, "If anyone thirsts, let him come to me and drink."
verses.notes: Pluperfect εἱστήκει. Third class condition ἐάν τις διψᾷ.

verses.greek: ὁ πιστεύων εἰς ἐμέ καθὼς εἶπεν ἡ γραφή ποταμοὶ ἐκ τῆς κοιλίας αὐτοῦ ῥεύσουσιν ὕδατος ζῶντος
verses.transliteration: ho pisteuōn eis eme kathōs eipen hē graphē potamoi ek tēs koilias autou reusousin hydatos zōntos
verses.referenceTranslation: "The one who believes in me, as the Scripture has said, out of his heart will flow rivers of living water."
verses.notes: Quotation formula καθὼς εἶπεν ἡ γραφή. Future ῥεύσουσιν. κοιλία — 'innermost being.'

verses.greek: Πάλιν οὖν αὐτοῖς ἐλάλησεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς λέγων ἐγώ εἰμι τὸ φῶς τοῦ κόσμου ὁ ἀκολουθῶν ἐμοὶ οὐ μὴ περιπατήσῃ ἐν τῇ σκοτίᾳ ἀλλ᾽ ἕξει τὸ φῶς τῆς ζωῆς
verses.transliteration: Palin oun autois elalēsen ho Iēsous legōn egō eimi to phōs tou kosmou ho akolouthōn emoi ou mē peripatēsē en tē skotia all hexei to phōs tēs zōēs
verses.referenceTranslation: Again, therefore, Jesus spoke to them, saying, “I am the light of the world. He who follows me will not walk in the darkness, but will have the light of life.”
verses.notes: Second 'I am' statement. Present participle ὁ ἀκολουθῶν. οὐ μή + subjunctive — emphatic negative.

verses.greek: Ἔλεγεν οὖν ὁ Ἰησοῦς πρὸς τοὺς πεπιστευκότας αὐτῷ Ἰουδαίους ἐὰν ὑμεῖς μείνητε ἐν τῷ λόγῳ τῷ ἐμῷ ἀληθῶς μαθηταί μού ἐστε
verses.transliteration: Elegen oun ho Iēsous pros tous pepisteukotas autō Ioudaious ean hymeis meinēte en tō logō tō emō alēthōs mathētai mou este
verses.referenceTranslation: Jesus therefore said to those Jews who had believed him, “If you remain in my word, then you are truly my disciples.
verses.notes: Third class condition ἐάν + subjunctive μείνητε. Adverb ἀληθῶς — 'truly.'

verses.greek: καὶ γνώσεσθε τὴν ἀλήθειαν, καὶ ἡ ἀλήθεια ἐλευθερώσει ὑμᾶς.
verses.transliteration: Kai gnōsesthe tēn alētheian, kai hē alētheia eleutherōsei humas.
verses.referenceTranslation: And you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.
verses.notes: Truth and freedom. Future tenses.

verses.greek: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι πᾶς ὁ ποιῶν τὴν ἁμαρτίαν δοῦλός ἐστιν τῆς ἁμαρτίας
verses.transliteration: apekrithē autois ho Iēsous amēn amēn legō hymin hoti pas ho poiōn tēn hamartian doulos estin tēs hamartias
verses.referenceTranslation: Jesus answered them, "Truly, truly, I say to you, everyone who practices sin is a slave to sin."
verses.notes: Present participle ὁ ποιῶν — habitual action. Predicate nominative δοῦλός ἐστιν.

verses.greek: ἐὰν οὖν ὁ υἱὸς ὑμᾶς ἐλευθερώσῃ ὄντως ἐλεύθεροι ἔσεσθε
verses.transliteration: ean oun ho uios hymas eleutherōsē ontōs eleutheroi esesthe
verses.referenceTranslation: If therefore the Son makes you free, you will be free indeed.
verses.notes: Third class condition ἐάν + subjunctive ἐλευθερώσῃ. Adverb ὄντως — 'really/indeed.'

verses.greek: ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ θύρα δι᾽ ἐμοῦ ἐάν τις εἰσέλθῃ σωθήσεται καὶ εἰσελεύσεται καὶ ἐξελεύσεται καὶ νομὴν εὑρήσει
verses.transliteration: egō eimi hē thyra di emou ean tis eiselthē sōthēsetai kai eiseleusetai kai exeleusetai kai nomēn eurēsei
verses.referenceTranslation: I am the door. If anyone enters in by me, he will be saved, and will go in and go out, and will find pasture.
verses.notes: 'I am' statement. Third class condition ἐάν τις εἰσέλθῃ. Three futures: σωθήσεται, εἰσελεύσεται, ἐξελεύσεται.

verses.greek: ὁ κλέπτης οὐκ ἔρχεται εἰ μὴ ἵνα κλέψῃ καὶ θύσῃ καὶ ἀπολέσῃ ἐγὼ ἦλθον ἵνα ζωὴν ἔχωσιν καὶ περισσὸν ἔχωσιν
verses.transliteration: ho kleptēs ouk erchetai ei mē hina klepsē kai thysē kai apolesē egō ēlthon hina zōēn echōsin kai perisson echōsin
verses.referenceTranslation: The thief comes only to steal, kill, and destroy. I came that they may have life, and have it abundantly.
verses.notes: Purpose clauses with ἵνα + subjunctive. περισσόν — 'abundantly/to the full.'

verses.greek: Ἐγώ εἰμι ὁ ποιμὴν ὁ καλός ὁ ποιμὴν ὁ καλὸς τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ τίθησιν ὑπὲρ τῶν προβάτων
verses.transliteration: Egō eimi ho poimēn ho kalos ho poimēn ho kalos tēn psychēn autou tithēsin hyper tōn probatōn
verses.referenceTranslation: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.
verses.notes: 'I am' statement. Present τίθησιν — 'lays down' voluntarily. ὑπέρ + genitive — on behalf of.

verses.greek: τὰ πρόβατα τὰ ἐμὰ τῆς φωνῆς μου ἀκούουσιν κἀγὼ γινώσκω αὐτὰ καὶ ἀκολουθοῦσίν μοι
verses.transliteration: ta probata ta ema tēs phōnēs mou akouousin kagō ginōskō auta kai akolouthousin moi
verses.referenceTranslation: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me.
verses.notes: Three present indicatives: ἀκούουσιν, γινώσκω, ἀκολουθοῦσιν — habitual actions.

verses.greek: κἀγὼ δίδωμι αὐτοῖς ζωὴν αἰώνιον καὶ οὐ μὴ ἀπόλωνται εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα καὶ οὐχ ἁρπάσει τις αὐτὰ ἐκ τῆς χειρός μου
verses.transliteration: kagō didōmi autois zōēn aiōnion kai ou mē apolōntai eis ton aiōna kai ouch harpasei tis auta ek tēs cheiros mou
verses.referenceTranslation: And I give them eternal life, and they shall never perish, and no one shall snatch them out of my hand.
verses.notes: οὐ μή + subjunctive — double emphatic negation. Future ἁρπάσει — 'snatch.'

verses.greek: ἐγὼ καὶ ὁ πατὴρ ἕν ἐσμεν
verses.transliteration: egō kai ho patēr hen esmen
verses.referenceTranslation: I and the Father are one.
verses.notes: Neuter ἕν (not masculine εἷς) — unity of essence, not person. First person plural ἐσμεν.

verses.greek: εἶπεν αὐτῇ ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ ἀνάστασις καὶ ἡ ζωή ὁ πιστεύων εἰς ἐμὲ κἂν ἀποθάνῃ ζήσεται
verses.transliteration: eipen autē ho Iēsous egō eimi hē anastasis kai hē zōē ho pisteuōn eis eme kan apothanē zēsetai
verses.referenceTranslation: Jesus said to her, "I am the resurrection and the life. The one who believes in me, even though he dies, will live."
verses.notes: 'I am' statement with double predicate. Concessive κἄν — 'even if.' Third class condition.

verses.greek: ἐδάκρυσεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς.
verses.transliteration: Edakrysen ho Iēsous.
verses.referenceTranslation: Jesus wept.
verses.notes: The shortest verse in the Bible. Ingressive aorist - 'burst into tears'.

verses.greek: κἀγὼ ἐὰν ὑψωθῶ ἐκ τῆς γῆς πάντας ἑλκύσω πρὸς ἐμαυτόν
verses.transliteration: kagō ean hypsōthō ek tēs gēs pantas helkysō pros emauton
verses.referenceTranslation: And I, if I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to myself.
verses.notes: Third class condition ἐάν + subjunctive ὑψωθῶ. Future ἑλκύσω — 'will draw.'

verses.greek: ἐγὼ φῶς εἰς τὸν κόσμον ἐλήλυθα ἵνα πᾶς ὁ πιστεύων εἰς ἐμὲ ἐν τῇ σκοτίᾳ μὴ μείνῃ
verses.transliteration: egō phōs eis ton kosmon elēlytha hina pas ho pisteuōn eis eme en tē skotia mē meinē
verses.referenceTranslation: I have come as a light into the world, so that everyone who believes in me should not remain in darkness.
verses.notes: Perfect ἐλήλυθα — abiding presence. Purpose clause ἵνα + subjunctive μείνῃ.

verses.greek: Ἐντολὴν καινὴν δίδωμι ὑμῖν ἵνα ἀγαπᾶτε ἀλλήλους καθὼς ἠγάπησα ὑμᾶς ἵνα καὶ ὑμεῖς ἀγαπᾶτε ἀλλήλους
verses.transliteration: Entolēn kainēn didōmi hymin hina agapate allēlous kathōs ēgapēsa hymas hina kai hymeis agapate allēlous
verses.referenceTranslation: A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another, just as I have loved you, so also you are to love one another.
verses.notes: Purpose clause ἵνα ἀγαπᾶτε. Comparative clause καθώς — 'just as I loved you.'

verses.greek: ἐν τούτῳ γνώσονται πάντες ὅτι ἐμοὶ μαθηταί ἐστε ἐὰν ἀγάπην ἔχητε ἐν ἀλλήλοις
verses.transliteration: en toutō gnōsontai pantes hoti emoi mathētai este ean agapēn echēte en allēlois
verses.referenceTranslation: By this all will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another.
verses.notes: Future γνώσονται. Third class condition ἐάν + subjunctive ἔχητε.

verses.greek: λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰησοῦς· ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ ὁδὸς καὶ ἡ ἀλήθεια καὶ ἡ ζωή· οὐδεὶς ἔρχεται πρὸς τὸν πατέρα εἰ μὴ δι᾽ ἐμοῦ.
verses.transliteration: Legei autō ho Iēsous: Egō eimi hē hodos kai hē alētheia kai hē zōē; oudeis erchetai pros ton patera ei mē di' emou.
verses.referenceTranslation: Jesus said to him, 'I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.'
verses.notes: One of the 'I am' statements. Note the emphatic ἐγώ εἰμι.

verses.greek: Ἐὰν ἀγαπᾶτέ με τὰς ἐντολὰς τὰς ἐμὰς τηρήσετε
verses.transliteration: Ean agapate me tas entolas tas emas tērēsete
verses.referenceTranslation: If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
verses.notes: Third class condition ἐάν + subjunctive ἀγαπᾶτε. Future indicative τηρήσετε.

verses.greek: ὁ ἔχων τὰς ἐντολάς μου καὶ τηρῶν αὐτὰς ἐκεῖνός ἐστιν ὁ ἀγαπῶν με ὁ δὲ ἀγαπῶν με ἀγαπηθήσεται ὑπὸ τοῦ πατρός μου κἀγὼ ἀγαπήσω αὐτὸν καὶ ἐμφανίσω αὐτῷ ἐμαυτόν
verses.transliteration: ho echōn tas entolas mou kai tērōn autas ekeinos estin ho agapōn me ho de agapōn me agapēthēsetai hypo tou patros mou kagō agapēsō auton kai emphanisō autō emauton
verses.referenceTranslation: One who has my commandments, and keeps them, that person is one who loves me. One who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love him, and will reveal myself to him.”
verses.notes: Substantival participles ὁ ἔχων, ὁ τηρῶν, ὁ ἀγαπῶν. Future ἐμφανίσω — 'I will reveal myself.'

verses.greek: ἀπεκρίθη Ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ἐάν τις ἀγαπᾷ με τὸν λόγον μου τηρήσει καὶ ὁ πατήρ μου ἀγαπήσει αὐτὸν καὶ πρὸς αὐτὸν ἐλευσόμεθα καὶ μονὴν παρ᾽ αὐτῷ ποιησόμεθα
verses.transliteration: apekrithē Iēsous kai eipen autō ean tis agapa me ton logon mou tērēsei kai ho patēr mou agapēsei auton kai pros auton eleusometha kai monēn par autō poiēsometha
verses.referenceTranslation: Jesus answered and said to him, "If anyone loves me, he will keep my word, and my Father will love him, and we will come to him and make our home with him."
verses.notes: Third class condition ἐάν τις ἀγαπᾷ. Future tenses: τηρήσει, ἀγαπήσει, ἐλευσόμεθα, ποιησόμεθα.

verses.greek: Εἰρήνην ἀφίημι ὑμῖν εἰρήνην τὴν ἐμὴν δίδωμι ὑμῖν οὐ καθὼς ὁ κόσμος δίδωσιν ἐγὼ δίδωμι ὑμῖν μὴ ταρασσέσθω ὑμῶν ἡ καρδία μηδὲ δειλιάτω
verses.transliteration: Eirēnēn aphiēmi hymin eirēnēn tēn emēn didōmi hymin ou kathōs ho kosmos didōsin egō didōmi hymin mē tarassesthō hymōn hē kardia mēde deiliatō
verses.referenceTranslation: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not your hearts be troubled, neither let them be afraid.
verses.notes: Present active ἀφίημι, δίδωμι. Negative imperatives μὴ ταρασσέσθω, μηδὲ δειλιάτω.

verses.greek: ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ ἄμπελος, ὑμεῖς τὰ κλήματα. ὁ μένων ἐν ἐμοὶ κἀγὼ ἐν αὐτῷ οὗτος φέρει καρπὸν πολύν.
verses.transliteration: Egō eimi hē ampelos, humeis ta klēmata. Ho menōn en emoi kagō en autō houtos pherei karpon polun.
verses.referenceTranslation: I am the vine; you are the branches. Whoever abides in me and I in him, he it is that bears much fruit.
verses.notes: Vine metaphor. Substantival participle (ὁ μένων).

verses.greek: Αὕτη ἐστὶν ἡ ἐντολὴ ἡ ἐμή ἵνα ἀγαπᾶτε ἀλλήλους καθὼς ἠγάπησα ὑμᾶς
verses.transliteration: Autē estin hē entolē hē emē hina agapate allēlous kathōs ēgapēsa hymas
verses.referenceTranslation: This is my commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you.
verses.notes: Demonstrative αὕτη — 'this is my commandment.' Purpose clause ἵνα ἀγαπᾶτε.

verses.greek: μείζονα ταύτης ἀγάπην οὐδεὶς ἔχει ἵνα τις τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ θῇ ὑπὲρ τῶν φίλων αὐτοῦ
verses.transliteration: meizona tautēs agapēn oudeis echei hina tis tēn psychēn autou thē hyper tōn philōn autou
verses.referenceTranslation: Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.
verses.notes: Comparative μείζονα. Purpose clause ἵνα + subjunctive θῇ. ψυχή — life/soul.

verses.greek: Ταῦτα λελάληκα ὑμῖν ἵνα ἐν ἐμοὶ εἰρήνην ἔχητε ἐν τῷ κόσμῳ θλῖψιν ἔχετε ἀλλὰ θαρσεῖτε ἐγὼ νενίκηκα τὸν κόσμον
verses.transliteration: Tauta lelalēka hymin hina en emoi eirēnēn echēte en tō kosmō thlipsin echete alla tharseite egō nenikēka ton kosmon
verses.referenceTranslation: I have told you these things, that in me you may have peace. In the world you have oppression; but cheer up! I have overcome the world.”
verses.notes: Perfect λελάληκα — lasting significance. Perfect νενίκηκα — permanent victory. Imperative θαρσεῖτε.

verses.greek: αὕτη δέ ἐστιν ἡ αἰώνιος ζωὴ ἵνα γινώσκωσιν σὲ τὸν μόνον ἀληθινὸν θεὸν καὶ ὃν ἀπέστειλας Ἰησοῦν Χριστόν
verses.transliteration: autē de estin hē aiōnios zōē hina ginōskōsin se ton monon alēthinon theon kai hon apesteilas Iēsoun Christon
verses.referenceTranslation: And this is eternal life, that they may know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ whom you have sent.
verses.notes: Present subjunctive γινώσκωσιν — experiential knowing. ἀληθινόν — 'genuine.'

verses.greek: ἁγίασον αὐτοὺς ἐν τῇ ἀληθείᾳ ὁ λόγος ὁ σὸς ἀλήθειά ἐστιν
verses.transliteration: hagiason autous en tē alētheia ho logos ho sos alētheia estin
verses.referenceTranslation: Sanctify them in the truth; your word is truth.
verses.notes: Aorist imperative ἁγίασον. Predicate nominative ἀλήθειά ἐστιν — 'Your word is truth.'

verses.greek: λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰησοῦς ὅτι ἑώρακάς με πεπίστευκας μακάριοι οἱ μὴ ἰδόντες καὶ πιστεύσαντες
verses.transliteration: legei autō ho Iēsous hoti heōrakas me pepisteukas makarioi oi mē idontes kai pisteusantes
verses.referenceTranslation: Jesus said to him, "Because you have seen me, you have believed. Blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed."
verses.notes: Perfect ἑώρακας, πεπίστευκας. Substantival participles οἱ μὴ ἰδόντες, πιστεύσαντες.

verses.greek: ταῦτα δὲ γέγραπται ἵνα πιστεύσητε ὅτι Ἰησοῦς ἐστιν ὁ χριστὸς ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ ἵνα πιστεύοντες ζωὴν ἔχητε ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι αὐτοῦ
verses.transliteration: tauta de gegraptai hina pisteusēte hoti Iēsous estin ho christos ho uios tou theou kai hina pisteuontes zōēn echēte en tō onomati autou
verses.referenceTranslation: but these are written, that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that believing you may have life in his name.
verses.notes: Perfect γέγραπται. Purpose clause ἵνα πιστεύσητε — either aorist (come to believe) or present (continue believing).

verses.greek: λέγει αὐτῷ τὸ τρίτον Σίμων Ἰωάννου φιλεῖς με ἐλυπήθη ὁ Πέτρος ὅτι εἶπεν αὐτῷ τὸ τρίτον φιλεῖς με καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ κύριε πάντα σὺ οἶδας σὺ γινώσκεις ὅτι φιλῶ σε λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰησοῦς βόσκε τὰ πρόβατά μου
verses.transliteration: legei autō to triton Simōn Iōannou phileis me elypēthē ho Petros hoti eipen autō to triton phileis me kai legei autō kyrie panta sy oidas sy ginōskeis hoti philō se legei autō ho Iēsous boske ta probata mou
verses.referenceTranslation: He said to him the third time, “Simon, son of Jonah, do you have affection for me?” Peter was grieved because he asked him the third time, “Do you have affection for me?” He said to him, “Lord, you know everything. You know that I have affection for you.” Jesus said to him, “Feed my sheep.
verses.notes: Distinction between ἀγαπᾷς (first two) and φιλεῖς (third). Aorist passive ἐλυπήθη.

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ λήμψεσθε δύναμιν ἐπελθόντος τοῦ ἁγίου πνεύματος ἐφ᾽ ὑμᾶς καὶ ἔσεσθέ μου μάρτυρες ἔν τε Ἰερουσαλὴμ καὶ ἐν πάσῃ τῇ Ἰουδαίᾳ καὶ Σαμαρείᾳ καὶ ἕως ἐσχάτου τῆς γῆς.
verses.transliteration: Alla lēmpsesthe dynamin epelthontos tou hagiou pneumatos eph' hymas kai esesthe mou martyres en te Ierousalēm kai en pasē tē Ioudaia kai Samareia kai heōs eschatou tēs gēs.
verses.referenceTranslation: But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you. You will be witnesses to me in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the uttermost parts of the earth.”
verses.notes: Programmatic verse for Acts. Genitive absolute ἐπελθόντος τοῦ ἁγίου πνεύματος.

verses.greek: Πέτρος δὲ πρὸς αὐτούς· μετανοήσατε, φησίν, καὶ βαπτισθήτω ἕκαστος ὑμῶν ἐπὶ τῷ ὀνόματι Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ εἰς ἄφεσιν τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν ὑμῶν καὶ λήμψεσθε τὴν δωρεὰν τοῦ ἁγίου πνεύματος.
verses.transliteration: Petros de pros autous: Metanoēsate, phēsin, kai baptisthētō hekastos hymōn epi tō onomati Iēsou Christou eis aphesin tōn hamartiōn hymōn kai lēmpsesthe tēn dōrean tou hagiou pneumatos.
verses.referenceTranslation: And Peter said to them, 'Repent and be baptized every one of you in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.'
verses.notes: Pentecost response. εἰς ἄφεσιν can be purpose or result.

verses.greek: Ἦσαν δὲ προσκαρτεροῦντες τῇ διδαχῇ τῶν ἀποστόλων καὶ τῇ κοινωνίᾳ τῇ κλάσει τοῦ ἄρτου καὶ ταῖς προσευχαῖς
verses.transliteration: Ēsan de proskarterountes tē didachē tōn apostolōn kai tē koinōnia tē klasei tou artou kai tais proseuchais
verses.referenceTranslation: They continued steadfastly in the apostles’ teaching and fellowship, in the breaking of bread, and prayer.
verses.notes: Periphrastic imperfect ἦσαν προσκαρτεροῦντες — continuous devotion. Four datives.

verses.greek: καὶ οὐκ ἔστιν ἐν ἄλλῳ οὐδενὶ ἡ σωτηρία οὐδὲ γὰρ ὄνομά ἐστιν ἕτερον ὑπὸ τὸν οὐρανὸν τὸ δεδομένον ἐν ἀνθρώποις ἐν ᾧ δεῖ σωθῆναι ἡμᾶς
verses.transliteration: kai ouk estin en allō oudeni hē sōtēria oude gar onoma estin heteron hypo ton ouranon to dedomenon en anthrōpois en ō dei sōthēnai hēmas
verses.referenceTranslation: And there is salvation in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven given among men by which we must be saved.
verses.notes: Emphatic negation οὐκ...οὐδενί...οὐδέ. Perfect passive participle δεδομένον.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ εἶπαν πίστευσον ἐπὶ τὸν κύριον Ἰησοῦν καὶ σωθήσῃ σὺ καὶ ὁ οἶκός σου
verses.transliteration: oi de eipan pisteuson epi ton kyrion Iēsoun kai sōthēsē sy kai ho oikos sou
verses.referenceTranslation: And they said, "Believe on the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved, you and your household."
verses.notes: Aorist imperative πίστευσον. Future passive σωθήσῃ. σύ and ὁ οἶκός σου — you and your household.

verses.greek: ἐν αὐτῷ γὰρ ζῶμεν καὶ κινούμεθα καὶ ἐσμέν ὡς καί τινες τῶν καθ᾽ ὑμᾶς ποιητῶν εἰρήκασιν τοῦ γὰρ καὶ γένος ἐσμέν
verses.transliteration: en autō gar zōmen kai kinoumetha kai esmen hōs kai tines tōn kath hymas poiētōn eirēkasin tou gar kai genos esmen
verses.referenceTranslation: For in him we live and move and have our being, as also some of your own poets have said, 'For we are indeed his offspring.'
verses.notes: Quotation from Epimenides/Aratus. Present indicatives ζῶμεν, κινούμεθα, ἐσμέν.

verses.greek: πάντα ὑπέδειξα ὑμῖν ὅτι οὕτως κοπιῶντας δεῖ ἀντιλαμβάνεσθαι τῶν ἀσθενούντων μνημονεύειν τε τῶν λόγων τοῦ κυρίου Ἰησοῦ ὅτι αὐτὸς εἶπεν μακάριόν ἐστιν μᾶλλον διδόναι ἢ λαμβάνειν
verses.transliteration: panta hypedeixa hymin hoti outōs kopiōntas dei antilambanesthai tōn asthenountōn mnēmoneuein te tōn logōn tou kyriou Iēsou hoti autos eipen makarion estin mallon didonai ē lambanein
verses.referenceTranslation: In all things I gave you an example, that so laboring you ought to help the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, that he himself said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’”
verses.notes: Aorist ὑπέδειξα. Present participles κοπιῶντας, ἀσθενούντων. Comparative μακάριόν.

verses.greek: Οὐ γὰρ ἐπαισχύνομαι τὸ εὐαγγέλιον, δύναμις γὰρ θεοῦ ἐστιν εἰς σωτηρίαν παντὶ τῷ πιστεύοντι, Ἰουδαίῳ τε πρῶτον καὶ Ἕλληνι.
verses.transliteration: Ou gar epaischynomai to euangelion, dynamis gar theou estin eis sōtērian panti tō pisteuonti, Ioudaiō te prōton kai Hellēni.
verses.referenceTranslation: For I am not ashamed of the gospel, for it is the power of God for salvation to everyone who believes, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
verses.notes: Theme verse of Romans. Note the substantival participle τῷ πιστεύοντι.

verses.greek: δικαιοσύνη γὰρ θεοῦ ἐν αὐτῷ ἀποκαλύπτεται ἐκ πίστεως εἰς πίστιν καθὼς γέγραπται ὁ δὲ δίκαιος ἐκ πίστεως ζήσεται
verses.transliteration: dikaiosynē gar theou en autō apokalyptetai ek pisteōs eis pistin kathōs gegraptai ho de dikaios ek pisteōs zēsetai
verses.referenceTranslation: For in it is revealed God’s righteousness from faith to faith. As it is written, “But the righteous shall live by faith.”
verses.notes: ἐκ πίστεως εἰς πίστιν — 'from faith to faith.' Habakkuk 2:4 quotation.

verses.greek: πάντες γὰρ ἥμαρτον καὶ ὑστεροῦνται τῆς δόξης τοῦ θεοῦ,
verses.transliteration: Pantes gar hēmarton kai hysterountai tēs doxēs tou theou,
verses.referenceTranslation: For all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God,
verses.notes: Key verse on universal sinfulness. Gnomic aorist ἥμαρτον.

verses.greek: Δικαιωθέντες οὖν ἐκ πίστεως εἰρήνην ἔχομεν πρὸς τὸν θεὸν διὰ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ
verses.transliteration: Dikaiōthentes oun ek pisteōs eirēnēn echomen pros ton theon dia tou kyriou hēmōn Iēsou Christou
verses.referenceTranslation: Being therefore justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ;
verses.notes: Aorist passive participle δικαιωθέντες. Present ἔχομεν (indicative) vs. ἔχωμεν (subjunctive) — textual variant.

verses.greek: συνίστησιν δὲ τὴν ἑαυτοῦ ἀγάπην εἰς ἡμᾶς ὁ θεός, ὅτι ἔτι ἁμαρτωλῶν ὄντων ἡμῶν Χριστὸς ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν ἀπέθανεν.
verses.transliteration: Synistēsin de tēn heautou agapēn eis hēmas ho theos, hoti eti hamartōlōn ontōn hēmōn Christos hyper hēmōn apethanen.
verses.referenceTranslation: But God shows his love for us in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us.
verses.notes: God's demonstration of love. Genitive absolute ἁμαρτωλῶν ὄντων ἡμῶν.

verses.greek: τὰ γὰρ ὀψώνια τῆς ἁμαρτίας θάνατος, τὸ δὲ χάρισμα τοῦ θεοῦ ζωὴ αἰώνιος ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ τῷ κυρίῳ ἡμῶν.
verses.transliteration: Ta gar opsōnia tēs hamartias thanatos, to de charisma tou theou zōē aiōnios en Christō Iēsou tō kyriō hēmōn.
verses.referenceTranslation: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
verses.notes: Contrast between sin's wages and God's gift. ὀψώνια is a military term for soldier's pay.

verses.greek: Οὐδὲν ἄρα νῦν κατάκριμα τοῖς ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ.
verses.transliteration: Ouden ara nun katakrima tois en Christō Iēsou.
verses.referenceTranslation: There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
verses.notes: No condemnation. Implied copula.

verses.greek: Λογίζομαι γὰρ ὅτι οὐκ ἄξια τὰ παθήματα τοῦ νῦν καιροῦ πρὸς τὴν μέλλουσαν δόξαν ἀποκαλυφθῆναι εἰς ἡμᾶς
verses.transliteration: Logizomai gar hoti ouk axia ta pathēmata tou nyn kairou pros tēn mellousan doxan apokalyphthēnai eis hēmas
verses.referenceTranslation: For I consider that the sufferings of the present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory that is to be revealed to us.
verses.notes: Present middle λογίζομαι. Articular infinitive τὴν μέλλουσαν...ἀποκαλυφθῆναι.

verses.greek: Ὡσαύτως δὲ καὶ τὸ πνεῦμα συναντιλαμβάνεται τῇ ἀσθενείᾳ ἡμῶν τὸ γὰρ τί προσευξώμεθα καθὸ δεῖ οὐκ οἴδαμεν ἀλλ᾽ αὐτὸ τὸ πνεῦμα ὑπερεντυγχάνει στεναγμοῖς ἀλαλήτοις
verses.transliteration: Hōsautōs de kai to pneuma synantilambanetai tē astheneia hēmōn to gar ti proseuxōmetha katho dei ouk oidamen all auto to pneuma hyperentynchanei stenagmois alalētois
verses.referenceTranslation: In the same way, the Spirit also helps our weaknesses, for we don’t know how to pray as we ought. But the Spirit himself makes intercession for us with groanings which can’t be uttered.
verses.notes: Compound verb συναντιλαμβάνεται — 'helps alongside.' ἀλάλητοι — 'wordless/inexpressible.'

verses.greek: οἴδαμεν δὲ ὅτι τοῖς ἀγαπῶσιν τὸν θεὸν πάντα συνεργεῖ εἰς ἀγαθόν, τοῖς κατὰ πρόθεσιν κλητοῖς οὖσιν.
verses.transliteration: Oidamen de hoti tois agapōsin ton theon panta synergei eis agathon, tois kata prothesin klētois ousin.
verses.referenceTranslation: And we know that for those who love God all things work together for good, for those who are called according to his purpose.
verses.notes: Divine providence. Substantival participle τοῖς ἀγαπῶσιν.

verses.greek: Τί οὖν ἐροῦμεν πρὸς ταῦτα εἰ ὁ θεὸς ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν τίς καθ᾽ ἡμῶν
verses.transliteration: Ti oun eroumen pros tauta ei ho theos hyper hēmōn tis kath hēmōn
verses.referenceTranslation: What then will we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?
verses.notes: Deliberative future ἐροῦμεν. First class condition εἰ + indicative — assumed true.

verses.greek: ἀλλ᾽ ἐν τούτοις πᾶσιν ὑπερνικῶμεν διὰ τοῦ ἀγαπήσαντος ἡμᾶς
verses.transliteration: all en toutois pasin hypernikōmen dia tou agapēsantos hēmas
verses.referenceTranslation: But in all these things we more than conquer through him who loved us.
verses.notes: Compound ὑπερνικῶμεν — 'we super-conquer.' Aorist participle τοῦ ἀγαπήσαντος.

verses.greek: πέπεισμαι γὰρ ὅτι οὔτε θάνατος οὔτε ζωὴ οὔτε ἄγγελοι οὔτε ἀρχαὶ οὔτε ἐνεστῶτα οὔτε μέλλοντα οὔτε δυνάμεις
verses.transliteration: pepeismai gar hoti oute thanatos oute zōē oute angeloi oute archai oute enestōta oute mellonta oute dynameis
verses.referenceTranslation: For I am persuaded that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor things present, nor things to come, nor powers,
verses.notes: Perfect passive πέπεισμαι — settled conviction. Catalogue of cosmic powers.

verses.greek: οὔτε ὕψωμα οὔτε βάθος οὔτε τις κτίσις ἑτέρα δυνήσεται ἡμᾶς χωρίσαι ἀπὸ τῆς ἀγάπης τοῦ θεοῦ τῆς ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ τῷ κυρίῳ ἡμῶν
verses.transliteration: oute hypsōma oute bathos oute tis ktisis hetera dynēsetai hēmas chōrisai apo tēs agapēs tou theou tēs en Christō Iēsou tō kyriō hēmōn
verses.referenceTranslation: nor height, nor depth, nor any other created thing, will be able to separate us from God’s love, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
verses.notes: Future δυνήσεται. Aorist infinitive χωρίσαι — 'to separate.' Climax of chapter 8.

verses.greek: ὅτι ἐὰν ὁμολογήσῃς ἐν τῷ στόματί σου κύριον Ἰησοῦν καὶ πιστεύσῃς ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ σου ὅτι ὁ θεὸς αὐτὸν ἤγειρεν ἐκ νεκρῶν σωθήσῃ
verses.transliteration: hoti ean homologēsēs en tō stomati sou kyrion Iēsoun kai pisteusēs en tē kardia sou hoti ho theos auton ēgeiren ek nekrōn sōthēsē
verses.referenceTranslation: That if you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord, and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
verses.notes: Third class condition ἐάν + subjunctive. Aorist ἤγειρεν — God raised Christ.

verses.greek: καρδίᾳ γὰρ πιστεύεται εἰς δικαιοσύνην στόματι δὲ ὁμολογεῖται εἰς σωτηρίαν
verses.transliteration: kardia gar pisteuetai eis dikaiosynēn stomati de homologeitai eis sōtērian
verses.referenceTranslation: For with the heart one believes unto righteousness, and with the mouth one confesses unto salvation.
verses.notes: Impersonal passives πιστεύεται, ὁμολογεῖται. Parallel structure: heart→righteousness, mouth→salvation.

verses.greek: ἄρα ἡ πίστις ἐξ ἀκοῆς ἡ δὲ ἀκοὴ διὰ ῥήματος Χριστοῦ
verses.transliteration: ara hē pistis ex akoēs hē de akoē dia rēmatos Christou
verses.referenceTranslation: So faith is from hearing, and hearing through the word of Christ.
verses.notes: ἄρα — inferential particle. ἐξ ἀκοῆς — faith comes 'from hearing.' Genitive Χριστοῦ — objective or subjective.

verses.greek: Ὦ βάθος πλούτου καὶ σοφίας καὶ γνώσεως θεοῦ ὡς ἀνεξεραύνητα τὰ κρίματα αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀνεξιχνίαστοι αἱ ὁδοὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.transliteration: Ō bathos ploutou kai sophias kai gnōseōs theou hōs anexeraunēta ta krimata autou kai anexichniastoi ai hodoi autou
verses.referenceTranslation: Oh the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and the knowledge of God! How unsearchable are his judgments, and his ways past tracing out!
verses.notes: Exclamatory Ὦ. Three genitives: πλούτου, σοφίας, γνώσεως. Alpha-privative ἀνεξεραύνητα.

verses.greek: Παρακαλῶ οὖν ὑμᾶς, ἀδελφοί, διὰ τῶν οἰκτιρμῶν τοῦ θεοῦ παραστῆσαι τὰ σώματα ὑμῶν θυσίαν ζῶσαν ἁγίαν εὐάρεστον τῷ θεῷ, τὴν λογικὴν λατρείαν ὑμῶν·
verses.transliteration: Parakalō oun hymas, adelphoi, dia tōn oiktirmōn tou theou parastēsai ta sōmata hymōn thysian zōsan hagian euareston tō theō, tēn logikēn latreian hymōn;
verses.referenceTranslation: I appeal to you therefore, brothers, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and acceptable to God, which is your spiritual worship.
verses.notes: Transition to ethical exhortation. λογικὴν can mean 'rational' or 'spiritual'.

verses.greek: καὶ μὴ συσχηματίζεσθε τῷ αἰῶνι τούτῳ ἀλλὰ μεταμορφοῦσθε τῇ ἀνακαινώσει τοῦ νοὸς εἰς τὸ δοκιμάζειν ὑμᾶς τί τὸ θέλημα τοῦ θεοῦ τὸ ἀγαθὸν καὶ εὐάρεστον καὶ τέλειον
verses.transliteration: kai mē syschēmatizesthe tō aiōni toutō alla metamorphousthe tē anakainōsei tou noos eis to dokimazein hymas ti to thelēma tou theou to agathon kai euareston kai teleion
verses.referenceTranslation: And do not be conformed to this age, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, so that you may prove what is the good, acceptable, and perfect will of God.
verses.notes: Present passive imperatives: μὴ συσχηματίζεσθε, μεταμορφοῦσθε. Articular infinitive τὸ δοκιμάζειν.

verses.greek: Ἡ ἀγάπη ἀνυπόκριτος ἀποστυγοῦντες τὸ πονηρόν κολλώμενοι τῷ ἀγαθῷ
verses.transliteration: Hē agapē anypokritos apostygountes to ponēron kollōmenoi tō agathō
verses.referenceTranslation: Let love be unfeigned: abhorring evil, cleaving to good,
verses.notes: Asyndeton — no connecting particles. ἀνυπόκριτος — 'without hypocrisy.' Substantival participles.

verses.greek: τῇ φιλαδελφίᾳ εἰς ἀλλήλους φιλόστοργοι τῇ τιμῇ ἀλλήλους προηγούμενοι
verses.transliteration: tē philadelphia eis allēlous philostorgoi tē timē allēlous proēgoumenoi
verses.referenceTranslation: In brotherly love, be devoted to one another; in honor, outdo one another.
verses.notes: Dative of sphere τῇ φιλαδελφίᾳ, τῇ τιμῇ. Present participle προηγούμενοι — 'outdoing one another.'

verses.greek: τῇ ἐλπίδι χαίροντες τῇ θλίψει ὑπομένοντες τῇ προσευχῇ προσκαρτεροῦντες
verses.transliteration: tē elpidi chairontes tē thlipsei hypomenontes tē proseuchē proskarterountes
verses.referenceTranslation: Rejoicing in hope, patient in tribulation, constant in prayer.
verses.notes: Three dative-participle pairs in parallel: ἐλπίδι/χαίροντες, θλίψει/ὑπομένοντες, προσευχῇ/προσκαρτεροῦντες.

verses.greek: μὴ νικῶ ὑπὸ τοῦ κακοῦ ἀλλὰ νίκα ἐν τῷ ἀγαθῷ τὸ κακόν
verses.transliteration: mē nikō hypo tou kakou alla nika en tō agathō to kakon
verses.referenceTranslation: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.
verses.notes: Present passive imperative νικῶ. Present active imperative νίκα. Chiastic structure.

verses.greek: Μηδενὶ μηδὲν ὀφείλετε εἰ μὴ τὸ ἀλλήλους ἀγαπᾶν ὁ γὰρ ἀγαπῶν τὸν ἕτερον νόμον πεπλήρωκεν
verses.transliteration: Mēdeni mēden opheilete ei mē to allēlous agapan ho gar agapōn ton heteron nomon peplērōken
verses.referenceTranslation: Owe nothing to anyone, except to love one another; for the one who loves the other has fulfilled the law.
verses.notes: Present imperative ὀφείλετε. εἰ μή — 'except.' Perfect πεπλήρωκεν — fully fulfilled.

verses.greek: Ὁ δὲ θεὸς τῆς ἐλπίδος πληρώσαι ὑμᾶς πάσης χαρᾶς καὶ εἰρήνης ἐν τῷ πιστεύειν εἰς τὸ περισσεύειν ὑμᾶς ἐν τῇ ἐλπίδι ἐν δυνάμει πνεύματος ἁγίου
verses.transliteration: Ho de theos tēs elpidos plērōsai hymas pasēs charas kai eirēnēs en tō pisteuein eis to perisseuein hymas en tē elpidi en dynamei pneumatos hagiou
verses.referenceTranslation: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that you may abound in hope, in the power of the Holy Spirit.
verses.notes: Optative πληρώσαι — prayer wish. εἰς τὸ περισσεύειν — purpose/result infinitive.

verses.greek: Ὁ λόγος γὰρ ὁ τοῦ σταυροῦ τοῖς μὲν ἀπολλυμένοις μωρία ἐστίν τοῖς δὲ σῳζομένοις ἡμῖν δύναμις θεοῦ ἐστιν
verses.transliteration: Ho logos gar ho tou staurou tois men apollymenois mōria estin tois de sōzomenois hēmin dynamis theou estin
verses.referenceTranslation: For the word of the cross is foolishness to those who are dying, but to us who are saved it is the power of God.
verses.notes: Substantival participles τοῖς ἀπολλυμένοις, τοῖς σῳζομένοις. Present tenses — ongoing process.

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ καθὼς γέγραπται ἃ ὀφθαλμὸς οὐκ εἶδεν καὶ οὖς οὐκ ἤκουσεν καὶ ἐπὶ καρδίαν ἀνθρώπου οὐκ ἀνέβη ἃ ἡτοίμασεν ὁ θεὸς τοῖς ἀγαπῶσιν αὐτόν
verses.transliteration: alla kathōs gegraptai ha ophthalmos ouk eiden kai ous ouk ēkousen kai epi kardian anthrōpou ouk anebē ha hētoimasen ho theos tois agapōsin auton
verses.referenceTranslation: But as it is written, "What no eye has seen, nor ear heard, nor the heart of man imagined, what God has prepared for those who love him."
verses.notes: Isaiah 64:4 allusion. Aorist εἶδεν, ἤκουσεν. Perfect ἡτοίμασεν in some mss.

verses.greek: ἢ οὐκ οἴδατε ὅτι τὸ σῶμα ὑμῶν ναὸς τοῦ ἐν ὑμῖν ἁγίου πνεύματός ἐστιν οὗ ἔχετε ἀπὸ θεοῦ καὶ οὐκ ἐστὲ ἑαυτῶν
verses.transliteration: ē ouk oidate hoti to sōma hymōn naos tou en hymin hagiou pneumatos estin ou echete apo theou kai ouk este heautōn
verses.referenceTranslation: Or do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit within you, whom you have from God? You are not your own.
verses.notes: Predicate nominative ναός. Genitive source ἀπὸ θεοῦ. Rhetorical question ἢ οὐκ οἴδατε.

verses.greek: ἠγοράσθητε γὰρ τιμῆς δοξάσατε δὴ τὸν θεὸν ἐν τῷ σώματι ὑμῶν
verses.transliteration: ēgorasthēte gar timēs doxasate dē ton theon en tō sōmati hymōn
verses.referenceTranslation: For you were bought with a price. Therefore glorify God in your body.
verses.notes: Aorist passive ἠγοράσθητε — purchased. Genitive of price τιμῆς. Aorist imperative δοξάσατε.

verses.greek: πειρασμὸς ὑμᾶς οὐκ εἴληφεν εἰ μὴ ἀνθρώπινος πιστὸς δὲ ὁ θεός ὃς οὐκ ἐάσει ὑμᾶς πειρασθῆναι ὑπὲρ ὃ δύνασθε ἀλλὰ ποιήσει σὺν τῷ πειρασμῷ καὶ τὴν ἔκβασιν τοῦ δύνασθαι ὑπενεγκεῖν
verses.transliteration: peirasmos hymas ouk eilēphen ei mē anthrōpinos pistos de ho theos hos ouk easei hymas peirasthēnai hyper ho dynasthe alla poiēsei syn tō peirasmō kai tēn ekbasin tou dynasthai hypenenkein
verses.referenceTranslation: No temptation has seized you except what is common to man. And God is faithful; he will not let you be tempted beyond what you can bear, but with the temptation will also provide the way of escape, so that you may be able to endure it.
verses.notes: Perfect εἴληφεν. εἰ μή — 'except.' Relative clause ὅς + future. Articular infinitive τὸ δύνασθαι.

verses.greek: Εἴτε οὖν ἐσθίετε εἴτε πίνετε εἴτε τι ποιεῖτε πάντα εἰς δόξαν θεοῦ ποιεῖτε
verses.transliteration: Eite oun esthiete eite pinete eite ti poieite panta eis doxan theou poieite
verses.referenceTranslation: Whether therefore you eat or drink, or whatever you do, do all things to the glory of God.
verses.notes: Conditional particles εἴτε...εἴτε...εἴτε — 'whether...or.' Present imperative ποιεῖτε.

verses.greek: Ὑμεῖς δέ ἐστε σῶμα Χριστοῦ καὶ μέλη ἐκ μέρους
verses.transliteration: Hymeis de este sōma Christou kai melē ek merous
verses.referenceTranslation: Now you are the body of Christ, and members individually.
verses.notes: Predicate nominative σῶμα Χριστοῦ. ἐκ μέρους — 'individually/each in part.'

verses.greek: Ἐὰν ταῖς γλώσσαις τῶν ἀνθρώπων λαλῶ καὶ τῶν ἀγγέλων ἀγάπην δὲ μὴ ἔχω γέγονα χαλκὸς ἠχῶν ἢ κύμβαλον ἀλαλάζον
verses.transliteration: Ean tais glōssais tōn anthrōpōn lalō kai tōn angelōn agapēn de mē echō gegona chalkos ēchōn ē kymbalon alalazon
verses.referenceTranslation: If I speak in the tongues of men and of angels, but have not love, I have become sounding brass or a clanging cymbal.
verses.notes: Third class condition ἐάν + subjunctive λαλῶ. Perfect γέγονα — 'I have become.'

verses.greek: Ἡ ἀγάπη μακροθυμεῖ, χρηστεύεται ἡ ἀγάπη, οὐ ζηλοῖ, ἡ ἀγάπη οὐ περπερεύεται, οὐ φυσιοῦται,
verses.transliteration: Hē agapē makrothymei, chrēsteuetai hē agapē, ou zēloi, hē agapē ou perpereuetai, ou physioutai,
verses.referenceTranslation: Love is patient and kind; love does not envy or boast; it is not arrogant
verses.notes: The love chapter. Series of present tense verbs describing love's character.

verses.greek: πάντα στέγει πάντα πιστεύει πάντα ἐλπίζει πάντα ὑπομένει
verses.transliteration: panta stegei panta pisteuei panta elpizei panta hypomenei
verses.referenceTranslation: Love bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
verses.notes: Four πάντα clauses — anaphoric repetition. Present indicatives — continuous action.

verses.greek: Ἡ ἀγάπη οὐδέποτε πίπτει εἴτε δὲ προφητεῖαι καταργηθήσονται εἴτε γλῶσσαι παύσονται εἴτε γνῶσις καταργηθήσεται
verses.transliteration: Hē agapē oudepote piptei eite de prophēteiai katargēthēsontai eite glōssai pausontai eite gnōsis katargēthēsetai
verses.referenceTranslation: Love never fails. But if there are prophecies, they will be done away with; if there are tongues, they will cease; if there is knowledge, it will be done away with.
verses.notes: Present πίπτει — love never falls. Future passives καταργηθήσονται, παύσονται.

verses.greek: Νυνὶ δὲ μένει πίστις ἐλπίς ἀγάπη τὰ τρία ταῦτα μείζων δὲ τούτων ἡ ἀγάπη
verses.transliteration: Nyni de menei pistis elpis agapē ta tria tauta meizōn de toutōn hē agapē
verses.referenceTranslation: But now abide faith, hope, and love, these three; but the greatest of these is love.
verses.notes: Present μένει. Comparative μείζων — 'greater/greatest.' τὰ τρία ταῦτα — 'these three things.'

verses.greek: παρέδωκα γὰρ ὑμῖν ἐν πρώτοις, ὃ καὶ παρέλαβον, ὅτι Χριστὸς ἀπέθανεν ὑπὲρ τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν ἡμῶν κατὰ τὰς γραφάς, καὶ ὅτι ἐτάφη, καὶ ὅτι ἐγήγερται τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τῇ τρίτῃ κατὰ τὰς γραφάς,
verses.transliteration: Paredōka gar hymin en prōtois, ho kai parelabon, hoti Christos apethanen hyper tōn hamartiōn hēmōn kata tas graphas, kai hoti etaphē, kai hoti egēgertai tē hēmera tē tritē kata tas graphas,
verses.referenceTranslation: For I delivered to you as of first importance what I also received: that Christ died for our sins in accordance with the Scriptures, that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day in accordance with the Scriptures,
verses.notes: Early Christian creed. Perfect tense ἐγήγερται emphasizes ongoing state of being raised.

verses.greek: παρέδωκα γὰρ ὑμῖν ἐν πρώτοις, ὃ καὶ παρέλαβον, ὅτι Χριστὸς ἀπέθανεν ὑπὲρ τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν ἡμῶν κατὰ τὰς γραφάς.
verses.transliteration: Paredōka gar humin en prōtois, ho kai parelabon, hoti Christos apethanen huper tōn hamartiōn hēmōn kata tas graphas.
verses.referenceTranslation: For I delivered to you as of first importance what I also received: that Christ died for our sins in accordance with the Scriptures.
verses.notes: Gospel summary. Technical terms for tradition (παρέδωκα, παρέλαβον).

verses.greek: καὶ ὅτι ἐτάφη καὶ ὅτι ἐγήγερται τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τῇ τρίτῃ κατὰ τὰς γραφὰς
verses.transliteration: kai hoti etaphē kai hoti egēgertai tē hēmera tē tritē kata tas graphas
verses.referenceTranslation: that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day according to the Scriptures,
verses.notes: Aorist passive ἐτάφη. Perfect passive ἐγήγερται — raised and remaining alive. κατὰ τὰς γραφάς.

verses.greek: χάριτι δὲ θεοῦ εἰμι ὅ εἰμι καὶ ἡ χάρις αὐτοῦ ἡ εἰς ἐμὲ οὐ κενὴ ἐγενήθη ἀλλὰ περισσότερον αὐτῶν πάντων ἐκοπίασα οὐκ ἐγὼ δὲ ἀλλ᾽ ἡ χάρις τοῦ θεοῦ ἡ σὺν ἐμοί
verses.transliteration: chariti de theou eimi ho eimi kai hē charis autou hē eis eme ou kenē egenēthē alla perissoteron autōn pantōn ekopiasa ouk egō de all hē charis tou theou hē syn emoi
verses.referenceTranslation: But by the grace of God I am what I am. His grace which was given to me was not futile, but I worked more than all of them; yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me.
verses.notes: Repeated χάρις (3x). Comparative περισσότερον — 'more abundantly.' οὐκ ἐγώ but ἡ χάρις.

verses.greek: ποῦ σου θάνατε τὸ νῖκος ποῦ σου θάνατε τὸ κέντρον
verses.transliteration: pou sou thanate to nikos pou sou thanate to kentron
verses.referenceTranslation: "Where, O death, is your victory? Where, O death, is your sting?"
verses.notes: Rhetorical taunt from Hosea 13:14. Vocative θάνατε. ποῦ — 'where?'

verses.greek: τῷ δὲ θεῷ χάρις τῷ διδόντι ἡμῖν τὸ νῖκος διὰ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ
verses.transliteration: tō de theō charis tō didonti hēmin to nikos dia tou kyriou hēmōn Iēsou Christou
verses.referenceTranslation: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.notes: Present participle τῷ διδόντι — God who keeps giving victory. διά + genitive — through Christ.

verses.greek: Γρηγορεῖτε στήκετε ἐν τῇ πίστει ἀνδρίζεσθε κραταιοῦσθε
verses.transliteration: Grēgoreite stēkete en tē pistei andrizesthe krataiousthe
verses.referenceTranslation: Be watchful, stand firm in the faith, act like men, be strong.
verses.notes: Four asyndetic imperatives: γρηγορεῖτε, στήκετε, ἀνδρίζεσθε, κραταιοῦσθε.

verses.greek: μὴ σκοπούντων ἡμῶν τὰ βλεπόμενα ἀλλὰ τὰ μὴ βλεπόμενα τὰ γὰρ βλεπόμενα πρόσκαιρα τὰ δὲ μὴ βλεπόμενα αἰώνια
verses.transliteration: mē skopountōn hēmōn ta blepomena alla ta mē blepomena ta gar blepomena proskaira ta de mē blepomena aiōnia
verses.referenceTranslation: while we don’t look at the things which are seen, but at the things which are not seen. For the things which are seen are temporal, but the things which are not seen are eternal.
verses.notes: Genitive absolute σκοπούντων ἡμῶν. Contrast: βλεπόμενα (visible) vs. μὴ βλεπόμενα (invisible), πρόσκαιρα vs. αἰώνια.

verses.greek: διὰ πίστεως γὰρ περιπατοῦμεν οὐ διὰ εἴδους
verses.transliteration: dia pisteōs gar peripatoumen ou dia eidous
verses.referenceTranslation: For we walk by faith, not by sight.
verses.notes: Genitive of means/sphere: διὰ πίστεως, διὰ εἴδους. Present περιπατοῦμεν — continuous walk.

verses.greek: ὥστε εἴ τις ἐν Χριστῷ, καινὴ κτίσις· τὰ ἀρχαῖα παρῆλθεν, ἰδοὺ γέγονεν καινά.
verses.transliteration: Hōste ei tis en Christō, kainē ktisis; ta archaia parēlthen, idou gegonen kaina.
verses.referenceTranslation: Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old has passed away; behold, the new has come.
verses.notes: New creation. Implied copula (ἐστίν). Perfect (γέγονεν) for permanent result.

verses.greek: τὸν μὴ γνόντα ἁμαρτίαν ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν ἁμαρτίαν ἐποίησεν ἵνα ἡμεῖς γενώμεθα δικαιοσύνη θεοῦ ἐν αὐτῷ
verses.transliteration: ton mē gnonta hamartian hyper hēmōn hamartian epoiēsen hina hēmeis genōmetha dikaiosynē theou en autō
verses.referenceTranslation: He made him who knew no sin to be sin on our behalf, so that we might become the righteousness of God in him.
verses.notes: Aorist participle γνόντα. Aorist ἐποίησεν. Purpose clause ἵνα γενώμεθα — great exchange.

verses.greek: ἕκαστος καθὼς προῄρηται τῇ καρδίᾳ μὴ ἐκ λύπης ἢ ἐξ ἀνάγκης ἱλαρὸν γὰρ δότην ἀγαπᾷ ὁ θεός
verses.transliteration: hekastos kathōs proērētai tē kardia mē ek lypēs ē ex anankēs hilaron gar dotēn agapa ho theos
verses.referenceTranslation: Each one as he purposes in his heart, not out of regret or of necessity, for God loves a cheerful giver.
verses.notes: Perfect middle προῄρηται. Proverbs 22:8 LXX allusion. ἱλαρόν — 'cheerful/hilarious.'

verses.greek: καὶ εἴρηκέν μοι ἀρκεῖ σοι ἡ χάρις μου ἡ γὰρ δύναμις ἐν ἀσθενείᾳ τελεῖται Ἥδιστα οὖν μᾶλλον καυχήσομαι ἐν ταῖς ἀσθενείαις μου ἵνα ἐπισκηνώσῃ ἐπ᾽ ἐμὲ ἡ δύναμις τοῦ Χριστοῦ
verses.transliteration: kai eirēken moi arkei soi hē charis mou hē gar dynamis en astheneia teleitai Hēdista oun mallon kauchēsomai en tais astheneiais mou hina episkēnōsē ep eme hē dynamis tou Christou
verses.referenceTranslation: And he said to me, "My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness." Therefore I will boast most gladly in my weaknesses, so that the power of Christ may rest upon me.
verses.notes: Perfect εἴρηκεν — permanent word from the Lord. Present passive τελεῖται — power perfected in weakness.

verses.greek: διὸ εὐδοκῶ ἐν ἀσθενείαις ἐν ὕβρεσιν ἐν ἀνάγκαις ἐν διωγμοῖς καὶ στενοχωρίαις ὑπὲρ Χριστοῦ ὅταν γὰρ ἀσθενῶ τότε δυνατός εἰμι
verses.transliteration: dio eudokō en astheneiais en hybresin en anankais en diōgmois kai stenochōriais hyper Christou hotan gar asthenō tote dynatos eimi
