verses.referenceTranslation: Therefore I take pleasure in weaknesses, in insults, in hardships, in persecutions, and difficulties for Christ; when for I might be weak, then strong. I am
verses.notes: Catalogue of sufferings. Paradox: ὅταν ἀσθενῶ, τότε δυνατός εἰμι.

verses.greek: ζῶ δὲ οὐκέτι ἐγώ, ζῇ δὲ ἐν ἐμοὶ Χριστός· ὃ δὲ νῦν ζῶ ἐν σαρκί, ἐν πίστει ζῶ τῇ τοῦ υἱοῦ τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ ἀγαπήσαντός με καὶ παραδόντος ἑαυτὸν ὑπὲρ ἐμοῦ.
verses.transliteration: Zō de ouketi egō, zē de en emoi Christos; ho de nyn zō en sarki, en pistei zō tē tou huiou tou theou tou agapēsantos me kai paradontos heauton hyper emou.
verses.referenceTranslation: I have been crucified with Christ, and it is no longer I that live, but Christ lives in me. That life which I now live in the flesh, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself up for me.
verses.notes: Union with Christ. Attributive participles τοῦ ἀγαπήσαντός... καὶ παραδόντος.

verses.greek: οὐκ ἔνι Ἰουδαῖος οὐδὲ Ἕλλην οὐκ ἔνι δοῦλος οὐδὲ ἐλεύθερος οὐκ ἔνι ἄρσεν καὶ θῆλυ πάντες γὰρ ὑμεῖς εἷς ἐστε ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ
verses.transliteration: ouk eni Ioudaios oude Hellēn ouk eni doulos oude eleutheros ouk eni arsen kai thēly pantes gar hymeis eis este en Christō Iēsou
verses.referenceTranslation: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither slave nor free man, there is neither male nor female; for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
verses.notes: Three pairs negated with οὐκ ἔνι. πάντες γὰρ ὑμεῖς εἷς ἐστε — 'you are all one.'

verses.greek: Τῇ ἐλευθερίᾳ ἡμᾶς Χριστὸς ἠλευθέρωσεν· στήκετε οὖν.
verses.transliteration: Tē eleutheria hēmas Christos ēleutherōsen; stēkete oun.
verses.referenceTranslation: Stand firm therefore in the liberty by which Christ has made us free, and don’t be entangled again with a yoke of bondage.
verses.notes: Freedom in Christ. Dative of advantage.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ καρπὸς τοῦ πνεύματός ἐστιν ἀγάπη, χαρά, εἰρήνη, μακροθυμία, χρηστότης, ἀγαθωσύνη, πίστις, πραΰτης, ἐγκράτεια· κατὰ τῶν τοιούτων οὐκ ἔστιν νόμος.
verses.transliteration: Ho de karpos tou pneumatos estin agapē, chara, eirēnē, makrothymia, chrēstotēs, agathōsynē, pistis, prautēs, enkrateia; kata tōn toioutōn ouk estin nomos.
verses.referenceTranslation: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, self-control; against such things there is no law.
verses.notes: The fruit of the Spirit. Singular καρπός with multiple manifestations.

verses.greek: πραΰτης ἐγκράτεια κατὰ τῶν τοιούτων οὐκ ἔστιν νόμος
verses.transliteration: prautēs enkrateia kata tōn toioutōn ouk estin nomos
verses.referenceTranslation: gentleness, self-control; against such things there is no law.
verses.notes: Final two fruit of the Spirit. κατὰ τῶν τοιούτων — 'against such things.' Litotes.

verses.greek: τὸ δὲ καλὸν ποιοῦντες μὴ ἐγκακῶμεν καιρῷ γὰρ ἰδίῳ θερίσομεν μὴ ἐκλυόμενοι
verses.transliteration: to de kalon poiountes mē enkakōmen kairō gar idiō therisomen mē eklyomenoi
verses.referenceTranslation: And let us not grow weary of doing good, for in due time we will reap a harvest, if we do not give up.
verses.notes: Hortatory subjunctive μὴ ἐγκακῶμεν. Future θερίσομεν — promise. Present participle ἐκλυόμενοι.

verses.greek: τῇ γὰρ χάριτί ἐστε σεσῳσμένοι διὰ πίστεως· καὶ τοῦτο οὐκ ἐξ ὑμῶν, θεοῦ τὸ δῶρον· οὐκ ἐξ ἔργων, ἵνα μή τις καυχήσηται.
verses.transliteration: Tē gar chariti este sesōsmenoi dia pisteōs; kai touto ouk ex hymōn, theou to dōron; ouk ex ergōn, hina mē tis kauchēsētai.
verses.referenceTranslation: For by grace you have been saved through faith. And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God, not a result of works, so that no one may boast.
verses.notes: Salvation by grace through faith. Periphrastic perfect ἐστε σεσῳσμένοι.

verses.greek: τῇ γὰρ χάριτί ἐστε σεσῳσμένοι διὰ πίστεως· καὶ τοῦτο οὐκ ἐξ ὑμῶν, θεοῦ τὸ δῶρον.
verses.transliteration: Tē gar chariti este sesōsmenoi dia pisteōs; kai touto ouk ex humōn, theou to dōron.
verses.referenceTranslation: For by grace you have been saved through faith. And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God.
verses.notes: Salvation by grace. Periphrastic perfect (ἐστε σεσῳσμένοι).

verses.greek: οὐκ ἐξ ἔργων ἵνα μή τις καυχήσηται
verses.transliteration: ouk ex ergōn hina mē tis kauchēsētai
verses.referenceTranslation: Not as a result of works, so that no one may boast.
verses.notes: Purpose clause ἵνα μή + subjunctive. Continuation of 2:8 — salvation not from works.

verses.greek: αὐτοῦ γάρ ἐσμεν ποίημα κτισθέντες ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ ἐπὶ ἔργοις ἀγαθοῖς οἷς προητοίμασεν ὁ θεός ἵνα ἐν αὐτοῖς περιπατήσωμεν
verses.transliteration: autou gar esmen poiēma ktisthentes en Christō Iēsou epi ergois agathois ois proētoimasen ho theos hina en autois peripatēsōmen
verses.referenceTranslation: For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand that we should walk in them.
verses.notes: ποίημα — 'masterpiece/workmanship.' Aorist passive participle κτισθέντες. Relative clause οἷς προητοίμασεν.

verses.greek: Τῷ δὲ δυναμένῳ ὑπὲρ πάντα ποιῆσαι ὑπερεκπερισσοῦ ὧν αἰτούμεθα ἢ νοοῦμεν κατὰ τὴν δύναμιν τὴν ἐνεργουμένην ἐν ἡμῖν
verses.transliteration: Tō de dynamenō hyper panta poiēsai hyperekperissou hōn aitoumetha ē nooumen kata tēn dynamin tēn energoumenēn en hēmin
verses.referenceTranslation: Now to him who is able to do exceedingly abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that works in us,
verses.notes: Compound adverb ὑπερεκπερισσοῦ — 'far beyond all measure.' Dative of reference κατὰ τὴν δύναμιν.

verses.greek: γίνεσθε δὲ εἰς ἀλλήλους χρηστοί, εὔσπλαγχνοι, χαριζόμενοι ἑαυτοῖς.
verses.transliteration: Ginesthe de eis allēlous chrēstoi, eusplanchnoi, charizomenoi heautois.
verses.referenceTranslation: Be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another.
verses.notes: Christian character. Present imperative + participle.

verses.greek: Οἱ ἄνδρες ἀγαπᾶτε τὰς γυναῖκας καθὼς καὶ ὁ Χριστὸς ἠγάπησεν τὴν ἐκκλησίαν καὶ ἑαυτὸν παρέδωκεν ὑπὲρ αὐτῆς
verses.transliteration: Oi andres agapate tas gynaikas kathōs kai ho Christos ēgapēsen tēn ekklēsian kai heauton paredōken hyper autēs
verses.referenceTranslation: Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ also loved the church and gave himself up for her,
verses.notes: Present imperative ἀγαπᾶτε — continuous love. Aorist ἠγάπησεν, παρέδωκεν — Christ's completed sacrifice.

verses.greek: Τοῦ λοιποῦ ἐνδυναμοῦσθε ἐν κυρίῳ καὶ ἐν τῷ κράτει τῆς ἰσχύος αὐτοῦ
verses.transliteration: Tou loipou endynamousthe en kyriō kai en tō kratei tēs ischyos autou
verses.referenceTranslation: Finally, be strong in the Lord and in the strength of his might.
verses.notes: Present passive imperative ἐνδυναμοῦσθε — 'keep being empowered.' Three synonyms: κράτος, ἰσχύς.

verses.greek: ἐνδύσασθε τὴν πανοπλίαν τοῦ θεοῦ πρὸς τὸ δύνασθαι ὑμᾶς στῆναι πρὸς τὰς μεθοδείας τοῦ διαβόλου
verses.transliteration: endysasthe tēn panoplian tou theou pros to dynasthai hymas stēnai pros tas methodeias tou diabolou
verses.referenceTranslation: Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the schemes of the devil.
verses.notes: Aorist imperative ἐνδύσασθε. πανοπλία — 'full armor/panoply.' Purpose infinitive τὸ δύνασθαι στῆναι.

verses.greek: ὅτι οὐκ ἔστιν ἡμῖν ἡ πάλη πρὸς αἷμα καὶ σάρκα ἀλλὰ πρὸς τὰς ἀρχάς πρὸς τὰς ἐξουσίας πρὸς τοὺς κοσμοκράτορας τοῦ σκότους τούτου πρὸς τὰ πνευματικὰ τῆς πονηρίας ἐν τοῖς ἐπουρανίοις
verses.transliteration: hoti ouk estin hēmin hē palē pros aima kai sarka alla pros tas archas pros tas exousias pros tous kosmokratoras tou skotous toutou pros ta pneumatika tēs ponērias en tois epouraniois
verses.referenceTranslation: For our wrestling is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the cosmic powers of this darkness, against spiritual forces of wickedness in the heavenly places.
verses.notes: Negative οὐκ...ἀλλά structure. Four πρός phrases listing spiritual enemies.

verses.greek: πεποιθὼς αὐτὸ τοῦτο ὅτι ὁ ἐναρξάμενος ἐν ὑμῖν ἔργον ἀγαθὸν ἐπιτελέσει ἄχρι ἡμέρας Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ
verses.transliteration: pepoithōs auto touto hoti ho enarxamenos en hymin ergon agathon epitelesei achri hēmeras Christou Iēsou
verses.referenceTranslation: Being confident of this very thing, that he who began a good work in you will bring it to completion until the day of Christ Jesus.
verses.notes: Perfect participle πεποιθώς. Aorist participle ἐναρξάμενος. Future ἐπιτελέσει.

verses.greek: Ἐμοὶ γὰρ τὸ ζῆν Χριστὸς καὶ τὸ ἀποθανεῖν κέρδος
verses.transliteration: Emoi gar to zēn Christos kai to apothanein kerdos
verses.referenceTranslation: To me, For to live is Christ and to die is gain.
verses.notes: Articular infinitives τὸ ζῆν, τὸ ἀποθανεῖν as subjects. Predicate nominatives Χριστός, κέρδος.

verses.greek: μηδὲν κατ᾽ ἐριθείαν μηδὲ κατὰ κενοδοξίαν, ἀλλὰ τῇ ταπεινοφροσύνῃ ἀλλήλους ἡγούμενοι ὑπερέχοντας ἑαυτῶν.
verses.transliteration: Mēden kat eritheian mēde kata kenodoxian, alla tē tapeinophrosunē allēlous hēgoumenoi hyperechontas heautōn.
verses.referenceTranslation: Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit, but in humility count others more significant than yourselves.
verses.notes: Humility. Accusative participle in indirect discourse.

verses.greek: τοῦτο φρονεῖτε ἐν ὑμῖν ὃ καὶ ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ, ὃς ἐν μορφῇ θεοῦ ὑπάρχων οὐχ ἁρπαγμὸν ἡγήσατο τὸ εἶναι ἴσα θεῷ, ἀλλ᾽ ἑαυτὸν ἐκένωσεν μορφὴν δούλου λαβών, ἐν ὁμοιώματι ἀνθρώπων γενόμενος·
verses.transliteration: Touto phroneite en hymin ho kai en Christō Iēsou, hos en morphē theou hyparchōn ouch harpagmon hēgēsato to einai isa theō, all' heauton ekenōsen morphēn doulou labōn, en homoiōmati anthrōpōn genomenos;
verses.referenceTranslation: Have this mind among yourselves, which is yours in Christ Jesus, who, though he was in the form of God, did not count equality with God a thing to be grasped, but emptied himself, by taking the form of a servant, being born in the likeness of men.
verses.notes: The Christ hymn. μορφῇ refers to essential nature, not mere appearance.

verses.greek: ἐταπείνωσεν ἑαυτὸν γενόμενος ὑπήκοος μέχρι θανάτου θανάτου δὲ σταυροῦ
verses.transliteration: etapeinōsen heauton genomenos hypēkoos mechri thanatou thanatou de staurou
verses.referenceTranslation: And being found in human form, he humbled himself, becoming obedient to death, yes, the death of the cross.
verses.notes: Aorist ἐταπείνωσεν. Present participle γενόμενος. Emphatic θανάτου δὲ σταυροῦ — 'even death on a cross.'

verses.greek: διὸ καὶ ὁ θεὸς αὐτὸν ὑπερύψωσεν καὶ ἐχαρίσατο αὐτῷ τὸ ὄνομα τὸ ὑπὲρ πᾶν ὄνομα
verses.transliteration: dio kai ho theos auton hyperypsōsen kai echarisato autō to onoma to hyper pan onoma
verses.referenceTranslation: Therefore God also highly exalted him, and gave to him the name which is above every name;
verses.notes: Compound verb ὑπερύψωσεν — 'super-exalted.' Aorist ἐχαρίσατο — 'graciously granted.'

verses.greek: ἵνα ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι Ἰησοῦ πᾶν γόνυ κάμψῃ ἐπουρανίων καὶ ἐπιγείων καὶ καταχθονίων
verses.transliteration: hina en tō onomati Iēsou pan gony kampsē epouraniōn kai epigeiōn kai katachthoniōn
verses.referenceTranslation: That at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, in the heavens and on earth and under the earth,
verses.notes: Purpose clause ἵνα + subjunctive κάμψῃ. Isaiah 45:23 allusion. Three spheres: ἐπουρανίων, ἐπιγείων, καταχθονίων.

verses.greek: καὶ πᾶσα γλῶσσα ἐξομολογήσηται ὅτι κύριος Ἰησοῦς Χριστὸς εἰς δόξαν θεοῦ πατρός
verses.transliteration: kai pasa glōssa exomologēsētai hoti kyrios Iēsous Christos eis doxan theou patros
verses.referenceTranslation: and that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
verses.notes: Subjunctive ἐξομολογήσηται. Climactic confession: κύριος Ἰησοῦς Χριστός.

verses.greek: ἀδελφοί ἐγὼ ἐμαυτὸν οὐ λογίζομαι κατειληφέναι ἓν δέ τὰ μὲν ὀπίσω ἐπιλανθανόμενος τοῖς δὲ ἔμπροσθεν ἐπεκτεινόμενος
verses.transliteration: adelphoi egō emauton ou logizomai kateilēphenai hen de ta men opisō epilanthanomenos tois de emprosthen epekteinomenos
verses.referenceTranslation: Brothers, I don’t regard myself as yet having taken hold, but one thing I do. Forgetting the things which are behind, and stretching forward to the things which are before,
verses.notes: Present middle λογίζομαι. Contrast: ὀπίσω ἐπιλανθανόμενος vs. ἔμπροσθεν ἐπεκτεινόμενος.

verses.greek: κατὰ σκοπὸν διώκω εἰς τὸ βραβεῖον τῆς ἄνω κλήσεως τοῦ θεοῦ ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ
verses.transliteration: kata skopon diōkō eis to brabeion tēs anō klēseōs tou theou en Christō Iēsou
verses.referenceTranslation: I press on toward the goal for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.
verses.notes: κατὰ σκοπόν — 'toward the mark/goal.' Athletic metaphor. Genitive τῆς ἄνω κλήσεως.

verses.greek: Χαίρετε ἐν κυρίῳ πάντοτε πάλιν ἐρῶ χαίρετε
verses.transliteration: Chairete en kyriō pantote palin erō chairete
verses.referenceTranslation: Rejoice in the Lord always; again I will say, rejoice!
verses.notes: Present imperative χαίρετε repeated. πάντοτε — 'at all times.' Emphatic repetition.

verses.greek: μηδὲν μεριμνᾶτε ἀλλ᾽ ἐν παντὶ τῇ προσευχῇ καὶ τῇ δεήσει μετὰ εὐχαριστίας τὰ αἰτήματα ὑμῶν γνωριζέσθω πρὸς τὸν θεόν
verses.transliteration: mēden merimnate all en panti tē proseuchē kai tē deēsei meta eucharistias ta aitēmata hymōn gnōrizesthō pros ton theon
verses.referenceTranslation: In nothing be anxious, but in everything, by prayer and petition with thanksgiving, let your requests be made known to God.
verses.notes: Present imperative μηδὲν μεριμνᾶτε. Three prayer terms: προσευχῇ, δεήσει, εὐχαριστίας.

verses.greek: καὶ ἡ εἰρήνη τοῦ θεοῦ ἡ ὑπερέχουσα πάντα νοῦν φρουρήσει τὰς καρδίας ὑμῶν καὶ τὰ νοήματα ὑμῶν ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ
verses.transliteration: kai hē eirēnē tou theou hē hyperechousa panta noun phrourēsei tas kardias hymōn kai ta noēmata hymōn en Christō Iēsou
verses.referenceTranslation: And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your thoughts in Christ Jesus.
verses.notes: Future φρουρήσει — military metaphor 'will garrison.' Comparative ὑπερέχουσα.

verses.greek: Τὸ λοιπόν ἀδελφοί ὅσα ἐστὶν ἀληθῆ ὅσα σεμνά ὅσα δίκαια ὅσα ἁγνά ὅσα προσφιλῆ ὅσα εὔφημα εἴ τις ἀρετὴ καὶ εἴ τις ἔπαινος ταῦτα λογίζεσθε
verses.transliteration: To loipon adelphoi hosa estin alēthē hosa semna hosa dikaia hosa hagna hosa prosphilē hosa euphēma ei tis aretē kai ei tis epainos tauta logizesthe
verses.referenceTranslation: Finally brothers, whatever is true, whatever is venerable, whatever is right, whatever is pure, whatever is lovely, whatever is admirable — if any excellence and if any praise — these things. Think on
verses.notes: Six ὅσα clauses. Present imperative λογίζεσθε — 'keep thinking about.' Virtue catalogue.

verses.greek: πάντα ἰσχύω ἐν τῷ ἐνδυναμοῦντί με.
verses.transliteration: Panta ischyō en tō endynamounti me.
verses.referenceTranslation: I can do all things through him who strengthens me.
verses.notes: Short but powerful. Substantival participle τῷ ἐνδυναμοῦντί.

verses.greek: πάντα ἰσχύω ἐν τῷ ἐνδυναμοῦντί με.
verses.transliteration: Panta ischuō en tō endunamounti me.
verses.referenceTranslation: I can do all things through him who strengthens me.
verses.notes: Christ-empowered living. Substantival participle (τῷ ἐνδυναμοῦντί).

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ θεός μου πληρώσει πᾶσαν χρείαν ὑμῶν κατὰ τὸ πλοῦτος αὐτοῦ ἐν δόξῃ ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ
verses.transliteration: ho de theos mou plērōsei pasan chreian hymōn kata to ploutos autou en doxē en Christō Iēsou
verses.referenceTranslation: My God will supply every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.
verses.notes: Future πληρώσει. κατὰ τὸ πλοῦτος — 'according to his riches' (not 'out of').

verses.greek: ὅς ἐστιν εἰκὼν τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ ἀοράτου, πρωτότοκος πάσης κτίσεως, ὅτι ἐν αὐτῷ ἐκτίσθη τὰ πάντα ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς καὶ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς, τὰ ὁρατὰ καὶ τὰ ἀόρατα,
verses.transliteration: Hos estin eikōn tou theou tou aoratou, prōtotokos pasēs ktiseōs, hoti en autō ektisthē ta panta en tois ouranois kai epi tēs gēs, ta horata kai ta aorata,
verses.referenceTranslation: He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of all creation. For by him all things were created, in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible,
verses.notes: Colossian hymn. πρωτότοκος indicates priority and sovereignty, not creation.

verses.greek: ὅτι ἐν αὐτῷ ἐκτίσθη τὰ πάντα ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς καὶ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς τὰ ὁρατὰ καὶ τὰ ἀόρατα εἴτε θρόνοι εἴτε κυριότητες εἴτε ἀρχαὶ εἴτε ἐξουσίαι τὰ πάντα δι᾽ αὐτοῦ καὶ εἰς αὐτὸν ἔκτισται
verses.transliteration: hoti en autō ektisthē ta panta en tois ouranois kai epi tēs gēs ta horata kai ta aorata eite thronoi eite kyriotētes eite archai eite exousiai ta panta di autou kai eis auton ektistai
verses.referenceTranslation: For by him all things were created, in the heavens and on the earth, things visible and things invisible, whether thrones or dominions or principalities or powers; all things have been created through him, and for him.
verses.notes: Aorist passive ἐκτίσθη. ἐν αὐτῷ — instrumental/locative. Four categories of powers.

verses.greek: καὶ αὐτός ἐστιν πρὸ πάντων καὶ τὰ πάντα ἐν αὐτῷ συνέστηκεν
verses.transliteration: kai autos estin pro pantōn kai ta panta en autō synestēken
verses.referenceTranslation: And He is before all things, and all things in Him hold together.
verses.notes: Present ἐστιν — eternal existence. Perfect συνέστηκεν — all things hold together (and continue to).

verses.greek: ὅτι ἐν αὐτῷ κατοικεῖ πᾶν τὸ πλήρωμα τῆς θεότητος σωματικῶς
verses.transliteration: hoti en autō katoikei pan to plērōma tēs theotētos sōmatikōs
verses.referenceTranslation: For in him all the fullness of the Deity dwells bodily,
verses.notes: Present κατοικεῖ — permanently dwells. θεότης (deity/Godhead) vs. θειότης (divinity).

verses.greek: Εἰ οὖν συνηγέρθητε τῷ Χριστῷ τὰ ἄνω ζητεῖτε οὗ ὁ Χριστός ἐστιν ἐν δεξιᾷ τοῦ θεοῦ καθήμενος
verses.transliteration: Ei oun synēgerthēte tō Christō ta anō zēteite ou ho Christos estin en dexia tou theou kathēmenos
verses.referenceTranslation: If then you have been raised with Christ, the things above, seek where Christ is at the right hand of God. Sitting
verses.notes: First class condition εἰ + indicative. Aorist passive συνηγέρθητε. Present imperative ζητεῖτε.

verses.greek: τὰ ἄνω φρονεῖτε μὴ τὰ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς
verses.transliteration: ta anō phroneite mē ta epi tēs gēs
verses.referenceTranslation: Set your minds on the things above, not on the things on the earth.
verses.notes: Present imperative φρονεῖτε — 'keep setting your mind on.' Contrast: τὰ ἄνω vs. τὰ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς.

verses.greek: Ἐνδύσασθε οὖν ὡς ἐκλεκτοὶ τοῦ θεοῦ ἅγιοι καὶ ἠγαπημένοι σπλάγχνα οἰκτιρμοῦ χρηστότητα ταπεινοφροσύνην πραΰτητα μακροθυμίαν
verses.transliteration: Endysasthe oun hōs eklektoi tou theou hagioi kai ēgapēmenoi splanchna oiktirmou chrēstotēta tapeinophrosynēn prautēta makrothymian
verses.referenceTranslation: Put on therefore, as God’s chosen ones, holy and beloved, a heart of compassion, kindness, lowliness, humility, and perseverance;
verses.notes: Aorist imperative ἐνδύσασθε — 'clothe yourselves.' Five virtues as garments.

verses.greek: ἀνεχόμενοι ἀλλήλων καὶ χαριζόμενοι ἑαυτοῖς ἐάν τις πρός τινα ἔχῃ μομφήν καθὼς καὶ ὁ κύριος ἐχαρίσατο ὑμῖν οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς
verses.transliteration: anechomenoi allēlōn kai charizomenoi heautois ean tis pros tina echē momphēn kathōs kai ho kyrios echarisato hymin outōs kai hymeis
verses.referenceTranslation: bearing with one another and forgiving each other; if anyone has a complaint against another, just as the Lord forgave you, so also should you.
verses.notes: Present participles ἀνεχόμενοι, χαριζόμενοι. καθώς — 'just as the Lord forgave.'

verses.greek: ὁ λόγος τοῦ Χριστοῦ ἐνοικείτω ἐν ὑμῖν πλουσίως.
verses.transliteration: Ho logos tou Christou enoikeitō en humin plousiōs.
verses.referenceTranslation: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly.
verses.notes: Christ's word indwelling. Present imperative (ἐνοικείτω).

verses.greek: καὶ πᾶν ὅ τι ἐὰν ποιῆτε ἐν λόγῳ ἢ ἐν ἔργῳ πάντα ἐν ὀνόματι κυρίου Ἰησοῦ εὐχαριστοῦντες τῷ θεῷ πατρὶ δι᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.transliteration: kai pan ho ti ean poiēte en logō ē en ergō panta en onomati kyriou Iēsou eucharistountes tō theō patri di autou
verses.referenceTranslation: Whatever you do, in word or in deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father, through him.
verses.notes: Indefinite relative ὅ τι ἐάν + subjunctive. Present participle εὐχαριστοῦντες.

verses.greek: ὃ ἐὰν ποιῆτε ἐκ ψυχῆς ἐργάζεσθε ὡς τῷ κυρίῳ καὶ οὐκ ἀνθρώποις
verses.transliteration: ho ean poiēte ek psychēs ergazesthe hōs tō kyriō kai ouk anthrōpois
verses.referenceTranslation: Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart, as working for the Lord and not for men,
verses.notes: ἐκ ψυχῆς — 'from the soul/heartily.' Present imperative ἐργάζεσθε. ὡς τῷ κυρίῳ — 'as unto the Lord.'

verses.greek: Τοῦτο γάρ ἐστιν θέλημα τοῦ θεοῦ ὁ ἁγιασμὸς ὑμῶν ἀπέχεσθαι ὑμᾶς ἀπὸ τῆς πορνείας
verses.transliteration: Touto gar estin thelēma tou theou ho hagiasmos hymōn apechesthai hymas apo tēs porneias
verses.referenceTranslation: For this is the will of God, your sanctification: that you abstain from sexual immorality;
verses.notes: Predicate nominative ὁ ἁγιασμός. Epexegetical infinitive ἀπέχεσθαι explains sanctification.

verses.greek: Πάντοτε χαίρετε, ἀδιαλείπτως προσεύχεσθε, ἐν παντὶ εὐχαριστεῖτε· τοῦτο γὰρ θέλημα θεοῦ ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ εἰς ὑμᾶς.
verses.transliteration: Pantote chairete, adialeiptōs proseuchesthe, en panti eucharisteite; touto gar thelēma theou en Christō Iēsou eis hymas.
verses.referenceTranslation: Rejoice always.
verses.notes: Three short imperatives summarizing Christian living.

verses.greek: ἀδιαλείπτως προσεύχεσθε
verses.transliteration: adialeiptōs proseuchesthe
verses.referenceTranslation: Pray unceasingly.
verses.notes: Shortest verse in Greek NT. Present imperative προσεύχεσθε — continuous prayer. Adverb ἀδιαλείπτως.

verses.greek: ἐν παντὶ εὐχαριστεῖτε τοῦτο γὰρ θέλημα θεοῦ ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ εἰς ὑμᾶς
verses.transliteration: en panti eucharisteite touto gar thelēma theou en Christō Iēsou eis hymas
verses.referenceTranslation: In everything give thanks, for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus toward you.
verses.notes: Present imperative εὐχαριστεῖτε. ἐν παντί — 'in every circumstance.'

verses.greek: Πιστὸς δέ ἐστιν ὁ κύριος ὃς στηρίξει ὑμᾶς καὶ φυλάξει ἀπὸ τοῦ πονηροῦ
verses.transliteration: Pistos de estin ho kyrios hos stērixei hymas kai phylaxei apo tou ponērou
verses.referenceTranslation: But the Lord is faithful, who will establish you, and guard you from the evil one.
verses.notes: Predicate adjective πιστός. Future verbs στηρίξει, φυλάξει — promises.

verses.greek: Εἷς γὰρ θεός εἷς καὶ μεσίτης θεοῦ καὶ ἀνθρώπων ἄνθρωπος Χριστὸς Ἰησοῦς
verses.transliteration: Eis gar theos eis kai mesitēs theou kai anthrōpōn anthrōpos Christos Iēsous
verses.referenceTranslation: For there is one God and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus,
verses.notes: Emphatic εἷς repeated. μεσίτης — 'mediator/go-between.' ἄνθρωπος — humanity of Christ.

verses.greek: Μηδείς σου τῆς νεότητος καταφρονείτω ἀλλὰ τύπος γίνου τῶν πιστῶν ἐν λόγῳ ἐν ἀναστροφῇ ἐν ἀγάπῃ ἐν πίστει ἐν ἁγνείᾳ
verses.transliteration: Mēdeis sou tēs neotētos kataphroneitō alla typos ginou tōn pistōn en logō en anastrophē en agapē en pistei en hagneia
verses.referenceTranslation: Let no one despise your youth, but be an example to the believers in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith, and in purity.
verses.notes: Present imperative καταφρονείτω with μηδείς. Present imperative γίνου. Five spheres: λόγῳ, ἀναστροφῇ, ἀγάπῃ, πίστει, ἁγνείᾳ.

verses.greek: Ἔστιν δὲ πορισμὸς μέγας ἡ εὐσέβεια μετὰ αὐταρκείας
verses.transliteration: Estin de porismos megas hē eusebeia meta autarkeias
verses.referenceTranslation: But godliness with contentment is great gain.
verses.notes: Predicate nominative πορισμὸς μέγας. εὐσέβεια μετὰ αὐταρκείας — godliness combined with contentment.

verses.greek: ῥίζα γὰρ πάντων τῶν κακῶν ἐστιν ἡ φιλαργυρία ἧς τινες ὀρεγόμενοι ἀπεπλανήθησαν ἀπὸ τῆς πίστεως καὶ ἑαυτοὺς περιέπειραν ὀδύναις πολλαῖς
verses.transliteration: riza gar pantōn tōn kakōn estin hē philargyria hēs tines oregomenoi apeplanēthēsan apo tēs pisteōs kai heautous periepeiran odynais pollais
verses.referenceTranslation: For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evils, which some, reaching after, have been led astray from the faith and have pierced themselves with many sorrows.
verses.notes: Predicate nominative ῥίζα πάντων τῶν κακῶν. φιλαργυρία — 'love of silver/money.'

verses.greek: ἀγωνίζου τὸν καλὸν ἀγῶνα τῆς πίστεως, ἐπιλαβοῦ τῆς αἰωνίου ζωῆς.
verses.transliteration: Agōnizou ton kalon agōna tēs pisteōs, epilabou tēs aiōniou zōēs.
verses.referenceTranslation: Fight the good fight of the faith. Take hold of the eternal life.
verses.notes: Good fight. Cognate accusative (ἀγῶνα).

verses.greek: οὐ γὰρ ἔδωκεν ἡμῖν ὁ θεὸς πνεῦμα δειλίας ἀλλὰ δυνάμεως καὶ ἀγάπης καὶ σωφρονισμοῦ
verses.transliteration: ou gar edōken hēmin ho theos pneuma deilias alla dynameōs kai agapēs kai sōphronismou
verses.referenceTranslation: For God has not given us a spirit of cowardice, but of power, love, and self-control.
verses.notes: Aorist ἔδωκεν. Three positive genitives: δυνάμεως, ἀγάπης, σωφρονισμοῦ vs. one negative: δειλίας.

verses.greek: σπούδασον σεαυτὸν δόκιμον παραστῆσαι τῷ θεῷ ἐργάτην ἀνεπαίσχυντον ὀρθοτομοῦντα τὸν λόγον τῆς ἀληθείας
verses.transliteration: spoudason seauton dokimon parastēsai tō theō ergatēn anepaischynton orthotomounta ton logon tēs alētheias
verses.referenceTranslation: Be diligent to present yourself approved to God, a workman not ashamed, rightly handling the word of truth.
verses.notes: Aorist imperative σπούδασον. ὀρθοτομοῦντα — 'cutting straight/rightly handling.'

verses.greek: πᾶσα γραφὴ θεόπνευστος καὶ ὠφέλιμος πρὸς διδασκαλίαν, πρὸς ἐλεγμόν, πρὸς ἐπανόρθωσιν, πρὸς παιδείαν τὴν ἐν δικαιοσύνῃ,
verses.transliteration: Pasa graphē theopneustos kai ōphelimos pros didaskalian, pros elegmon, pros epanorthōsin, pros paideian tēn en dikaiosynē,
verses.referenceTranslation: All Scripture is breathed out by God and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness,
verses.notes: Inspiration of Scripture. θεόπνευστος is a compound: θεός + πνέω.

verses.greek: ἵνα ἄρτιος ᾖ ὁ τοῦ θεοῦ ἄνθρωπος πρὸς πᾶν ἔργον ἀγαθὸν ἐξηρτισμένος
verses.transliteration: hina artios ē ho tou theou anthrōpos pros pan ergon agathon exērtismenos
verses.referenceTranslation: so that the man of God may be complete, equipped for every good work.
verses.notes: Purpose clause ἵνα + subjunctive ᾖ. ἄρτιος — 'complete/capable.' Perfect passive ἐξηρτισμένος.

verses.greek: τὸν καλὸν ἀγῶνα ἠγώνισμαι τὸν δρόμον τετέλεκα τὴν πίστιν τετήρηκα
verses.transliteration: ton kalon agōna ēgōnismai ton dromon teteleka tēn pistin tetērēka
verses.referenceTranslation: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
verses.notes: Three perfect verbs: ἠγώνισμαι, τετέλεκα, τετήρηκα — completed with lasting result. Athletic metaphor.

verses.greek: Ἐπεφάνη γὰρ ἡ χάρις τοῦ θεοῦ σωτήριος πᾶσιν ἀνθρώποις
verses.transliteration: Epephanē gar hē charis tou theou sōtērios pasin anthrōpois
verses.referenceTranslation: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all men,
verses.notes: Aorist passive ἐπεφάνη — the grace of God appeared. σωτήριος — 'bringing salvation.'

verses.greek: οὐκ ἐξ ἔργων τῶν ἐν δικαιοσύνῃ ἃ ἐποιήσαμεν ἡμεῖς ἀλλὰ κατὰ τὸ αὐτοῦ ἔλεος ἔσωσεν ἡμᾶς διὰ λουτροῦ παλιγγενεσίας καὶ ἀνακαινώσεως πνεύματος ἁγίου
verses.transliteration: ouk ex ergōn tōn en dikaiosynē ha epoiēsamen hēmeis alla kata to autou eleos esōsen hēmas dia loutrou palingenesias kai anakainōseōs pneumatos hagiou
verses.referenceTranslation: not by works of righteousness which we did ourselves, but according to his mercy, he saved us through the washing of regeneration and renewing by the Holy Spirit,
verses.notes: Aorist ἔσωσεν. Two genitives: παλιγγενεσίας (regeneration), ἀνακαινώσεως (renewal). Not by works but by mercy.

verses.greek: Πολυμερῶς καὶ πολυτρόπως πάλαι ὁ θεὸς λαλήσας τοῖς πατράσιν ἐν τοῖς προφήταις ἐπ᾽ ἐσχάτου τῶν ἡμερῶν τούτων ἐλάλησεν ἡμῖν ἐν υἱῷ,
verses.transliteration: Polymerōs kai polytropōs palai ho theos lalēsas tois patrasin en tois prophētais ep' eschatou tōn hēmerōn toutōn elalēsen hēmin en huiō,
verses.referenceTranslation: Long ago, at many times and in many ways, God spoke to our fathers by the prophets, but in these last days he has spoken to us by his Son,
verses.notes: Elegant Greek prologue. Alliterative πολυμερῶς καὶ πολυτρόπως.

verses.greek: Ζῶν γὰρ ὁ λόγος τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ ἐνεργὴς καὶ τομώτερος ὑπὲρ πᾶσαν μάχαιραν δίστομον καὶ διϊκνούμενος ἄχρι μερισμοῦ ψυχῆς καὶ πνεύματος ἁρμῶν τε καὶ μυελῶν καὶ κριτικὸς ἐνθυμήσεων καὶ ἐννοιῶν καρδίας
verses.transliteration: Zōn gar ho logos tou theou kai energēs kai tomōteros hyper pasan machairan distomon kai diiknoumenos achri merismou psychēs kai pneumatos harmōn te kai myelōn kai kritikos enthymēseōn kai ennoiōn kardias
verses.referenceTranslation: For the word of God is living and active, and sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing even to the dividing of soul and spirit, of both joints and marrow, and is able to discern the thoughts and intentions of the heart.
verses.notes: Predicate adjectives ζῶν, ἐνεργής. Comparative τομώτερος. Four penetration terms.

verses.greek: προσερχώμεθα οὖν μετὰ παρρησίας τῷ θρόνῳ τῆς χάριτος ἵνα λάβωμεν ἔλεος καὶ χάριν εὕρωμεν εἰς εὔκαιρον βοήθειαν
verses.transliteration: proserchōmetha oun meta parrēsias tō thronō tēs charitos hina labōmen eleos kai charin eurōmen eis eukairon boētheian
verses.referenceTranslation: Let us therefore draw near with boldness to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy and find grace to help in time of need.
verses.notes: Hortatory subjunctive προσερχώμεθα. Purpose clause ἵνα λάβωμεν...εὕρωμεν.

verses.greek: μὴ ἐγκαταλείποντες τὴν ἐπισυναγωγὴν ἑαυτῶν καθὼς ἔθος τισίν ἀλλὰ παρακαλοῦντες καὶ τοσούτῳ μᾶλλον ὅσῳ βλέπετε ἐγγίζουσαν τὴν ἡμέραν
verses.transliteration: mē enkataleipontes tēn episynagōgēn heautōn kathōs ethos tisin alla parakalountes kai tosoutō mallon hosō blepete engizousan tēn hēmeran
verses.referenceTranslation: Not forsaking the assembling together of ourselves as is the custom with some, but encouraging one another, and so much more as you see drawing near. The Day
verses.notes: Present participle ἐγκαταλείποντες with μή — prohibition. Present participle παρακαλοῦντες — ongoing encouragement.

verses.greek: Ἔστιν δὲ πίστις ἐλπιζομένων ὑπόστασις, πραγμάτων ἔλεγχος οὐ βλεπομένων.
verses.transliteration: Estin de pistis elpizomenōn hypostasis, pragmatōn elegchos ou blepomenōn.
verses.referenceTranslation: Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen.
verses.notes: Definition of faith. ὑπόστασις can mean 'substance', 'assurance', or 'reality'.

verses.greek: χωρὶς δὲ πίστεως ἀδύνατον εὐαρεστῆσαι πιστεῦσαι γὰρ δεῖ τὸν προσερχόμενον τῷ θεῷ ὅτι ἔστιν καὶ τοῖς ἐκζητοῦσιν αὐτὸν μισθαποδότης γίνεται
verses.transliteration: chōris de pisteōs adynaton euarestēsai pisteusai gar dei ton proserchomenon tō theō hoti estin kai tois ekzētousin auton misthapodotēs ginetai
verses.referenceTranslation: Without faith it is impossible to be well pleasing to him, for he who comes to God must believe that he exists, and that he is a rewarder of those who seek him.
verses.notes: Aorist infinitive εὐαρεστῆσαι. Two ὅτι clauses: God exists and rewards seekers.

verses.greek: τοιγαροῦν καὶ ἡμεῖς, τοσοῦτον ἔχοντες περικείμενον ἡμῖν νέφος μαρτύρων, ὄγκον ἀποθέμενοι πάντα καὶ τὴν εὐπερίστατον ἁμαρτίαν, δι᾽ ὑπομονῆς τρέχωμεν τὸν προκείμενον ἡμῖν ἀγῶνα, ἀφορῶντες εἰς τὸν τῆς πίστεως ἀρχηγὸν καὶ τελειωτὴν Ἰησοῦν,
verses.transliteration: Toigaroun kai hēmeis, tosouton echontes perikeimenon hēmin nephos martyrōn, ogkon apothemenoi panta kai tēn euperistaton hamartian, di' hypomonēs trechōmen ton prokeimenon hēmin agōna, aphorontes eis ton tēs pisteōs archēgon kai teleiōtēn Iēsoun,
verses.referenceTranslation: Therefore, since we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, let us also lay aside every weight, and sin which clings so closely, and let us run with endurance the race that is set before us, looking to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of our faith,
verses.notes: Athletic imagery. νέφος μαρτύρων is a striking metaphor.

verses.greek: ἀφορῶντες εἰς τὸν τῆς πίστεως ἀρχηγὸν καὶ τελειωτὴν Ἰησοῦν.
verses.transliteration: Aphorōntes eis ton tēs pisteōs archēgon kai teleiōtēn Iēsoun.
verses.referenceTranslation: looking to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising its shame, and has sat down at the right hand of the throne of God.
verses.notes: Focus on Jesus. Genitive attributive (τῆς πίστεως).

verses.greek: Ἀφιλάργυρος ὁ τρόπος ἀρκούμενοι τοῖς παροῦσιν αὐτὸς γὰρ εἴρηκεν οὐ μή σε ἀνῶ οὐδ᾽ οὐ μή σε ἐγκαταλίπω
verses.transliteration: Aphilargyros ho tropos arkoumenoi tois parousin autos gar eirēken ou mē se anō oud ou mē se enkatalipō
verses.referenceTranslation: Let your way of life be free from the love of money, being content with what you have; for he himself has said, "I will never leave you, nor will I ever forsake you."
verses.notes: ἀφιλάργυρος — 'free from love of money.' Double negative οὐ μή...οὐδ᾽ οὐ μή — emphatic promise.

verses.greek: Ἰησοῦς Χριστὸς ἐχθὲς καὶ σήμερον ὁ αὐτὸς καὶ εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας
verses.transliteration: Iēsous Christos echthes kai sēmeron ho autos kai eis tous aiōnas
verses.referenceTranslation: Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
verses.notes: Predicate adjective ὁ αὐτός — 'the same.' Three temporal markers: ἐχθές, σήμερον, εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας.

verses.greek: Πᾶσαν χαρὰν ἡγήσασθε, ἀδελφοί μου, ὅταν πειρασμοῖς περιπέσητε ποικίλοις, γινώσκοντες ὅτι τὸ δοκίμιον ὑμῶν τῆς πίστεως κατεργάζεται ὑπομονήν.
verses.transliteration: Pasan charan hēgēsasthe, adelphoi mou, hotan peirasmois peripesēte poikilois, ginōskontes hoti to dokimion hymōn tēs pisteōs katergazetai hypomonēn.
verses.referenceTranslation: Count it all joy, my brothers, when you meet trials of various kinds, for you know that the testing of your faith produces steadfastness.
verses.notes: Joy in trials. Aorist imperative ἡγήσασθε for decisive action.

verses.greek: Εἰ δέ τις ὑμῶν λείπεται σοφίας αἰτείτω παρὰ τοῦ διδόντος θεοῦ πᾶσιν ἁπλῶς καὶ μὴ ὀνειδίζοντος καὶ δοθήσεται αὐτῷ
verses.transliteration: Ei de tis hymōn leipetai sophias aiteitō para tou didontos theou pasin haplōs kai mē oneidizontos kai dothēsetai autō
verses.referenceTranslation: But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all liberally and without reproach; and it will be given to him.
verses.notes: First class condition εἰ + indicative. Present imperative αἰτείτω. ἁπλῶς — 'generously/without reserve.'

verses.greek: πᾶσα δόσις ἀγαθὴ καὶ πᾶν δώρημα τέλειον ἄνωθέν ἐστιν καταβαῖνον ἀπὸ τοῦ πατρὸς τῶν φώτων παρ᾽ ᾧ οὐκ ἔνι παραλλαγὴ ἢ τροπῆς ἀποσκίασμα
verses.transliteration: pasa dosis agathē kai pan dōrēma teleion anōthen estin katabainon apo tou patros tōn phōtōn par ō ouk eni parallagē ē tropēs aposkiasma
verses.referenceTranslation: Every good act of giving and every perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights, with whom there is no variation or shadow due to change.
verses.notes: Predicate adjective ἄνωθέν ἐστιν. 'Father of lights' — Creator of heavenly bodies. παραλλαγή, τροπῆς ἀποσκίασμα — astronomical metaphors.

verses.greek: Γίνεσθε δὲ ποιηταὶ λόγου καὶ μὴ μόνον ἀκροαταὶ παραλογιζόμενοι ἑαυτούς.
verses.transliteration: Ginesthe de poiētai logou kai mē monon akroatai paralogizomenoi heautous.
verses.referenceTranslation: But be doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving yourselves.
verses.notes: Faith and works. Present imperative γίνεσθε for ongoing action.

verses.greek: οὕτως καὶ ἡ πίστις ἐὰν μὴ ἔχῃ ἔργα νεκρά ἐστιν καθ᾽ ἑαυτήν
verses.transliteration: outōs kai hē pistis ean mē echē erga nekra estin kath heautēn
verses.referenceTranslation: So also faith, if it does not have works, is dead by itself.
verses.notes: Third class condition ἐάν μή. Predicate adjective νεκρά. καθ᾽ ἑαυτήν — 'by itself.'

verses.greek: ὑποτάγητε οὖν τῷ θεῷ ἀντίστητε δὲ τῷ διαβόλῳ καὶ φεύξεται ἀφ᾽ ὑμῶν
verses.transliteration: hypotagēte oun tō theō antistēte de tō diabolō kai pheuxetai aph hymōn
verses.referenceTranslation: Be subject therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
verses.notes: Aorist passive imperative ὑποτάγητε. Aorist imperative ἀντίστητε. Future φεύξεται — promise.

verses.greek: ἐξομολογεῖσθε οὖν ἀλλήλοις τὰς ἁμαρτίας καὶ εὔχεσθε ὑπὲρ ἀλλήλων ὅπως ἰαθῆτε πολὺ ἰσχύει δέησις δικαίου ἐνεργουμένη
verses.transliteration: exomologeisthe oun allēlois tas hamartias kai euchesthe hyper allēlōn hopōs iathēte poly ischyei deēsis dikaiou energoumenē
verses.referenceTranslation: Therefore confess your sins to one another, and pray for one another, so that you may be healed. The effective prayer of a righteous person avails much.
verses.notes: Present imperative ἐξομολογεῖσθε. Present imperative εὔχεσθε. Purpose clause ὅπως ἰαθῆτε.

verses.greek: ὑμεῖς δὲ γένος ἐκλεκτόν, βασίλειον ἱεράτευμα, ἔθνος ἅγιον, λαὸς εἰς περιποίησιν, ὅπως τὰς ἀρετὰς ἐξαγγείλητε τοῦ ἐκ σκότους ὑμᾶς καλέσαντος εἰς τὸ θαυμαστὸν αὐτοῦ φῶς·
verses.transliteration: Hymeis de genos eklekton, basileion hierateuma, ethnos hagion, laos eis peripoiēsin, hopōs tas aretas exangeilēte tou ek skotous hymas kalesantos eis to thaumaston autou phōs;
verses.referenceTranslation: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for his own possession, that you may proclaim the excellencies of him who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light.
verses.notes: OT allusions to Exodus 19:6 and Isaiah 43:20-21.

verses.greek: ὃς τὰς ἁμαρτίας ἡμῶν αὐτὸς ἀνήνεγκεν ἐν τῷ σώματι αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ τὸ ξύλον ἵνα ταῖς ἁμαρτίαις ἀπογενόμενοι τῇ δικαιοσύνῃ ζήσωμεν οὗ τῷ μώλωπι ἰάθητε
verses.transliteration: hos tas hamartias hēmōn autos anēnenken en tō sōmati autou epi to xylon hina tais hamartiais apogenomenoi tē dikaiosynē zēsōmen ou tō mōlōpi iathēte
verses.referenceTranslation: He himself bore our sins in his body on the tree, that we, having died to sins, might live to righteousness; by whose stripes you were healed.
verses.notes: Aorist ἀνήνεγκεν — 'carried up.' ξύλον — 'tree/wood' (= cross). Isaiah 53 allusion.

verses.greek: κύριον δὲ τὸν Χριστὸν ἁγιάσατε ἐν ταῖς καρδίαις ὑμῶν ἕτοιμοι ἀεὶ πρὸς ἀπολογίαν παντὶ τῷ αἰτοῦντι ὑμᾶς λόγον περὶ τῆς ἐν ὑμῖν ἐλπίδος
verses.transliteration: kyrion de ton Christon hagiasate en tais kardiais hymōn hetoimoi aei pros apologian panti tō aitounti hymas logon peri tēs en hymin elpidos
verses.referenceTranslation: But sanctify the Lord God in your hearts; and always be ready to give an answer to everyone who asks you a reason concerning the hope that is in you, with humility and fear:
verses.notes: Aorist imperative ἁγιάσατε. ἀπολογία — 'defense/answer.' Dative of advantage παντί.

verses.greek: πρὸ πάντων τὴν εἰς ἑαυτοὺς ἀγάπην ἐκτενῆ ἔχοντες ὅτι ἀγάπη καλύπτει πλῆθος ἁμαρτιῶν
verses.transliteration: pro pantōn tēn eis heautous agapēn ektenē echontes hoti agapē kalyptei plēthos hamartiōn
verses.referenceTranslation: Above all, keep your love for one another fervent, because love covers a multitude of sins.
verses.notes: Adjective ἐκτενῆ — 'stretched out/fervent.' Proverbs 10:12 allusion. Present καλύπτει.

verses.greek: πᾶσαν τὴν μέριμναν ὑμῶν ἐπιρίψαντες ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν, ὅτι αὐτῷ μέλει περὶ ὑμῶν.
verses.transliteration: Pasan tēn merimnan humōn epiripsantes ep auton, hoti autō melei peri humōn.
verses.referenceTranslation: Casting all your anxieties on him, because he cares for you.
verses.notes: Casting cares. Aorist participle (ἐπιρίψαντες).

verses.greek: νήψατε γρηγορήσατε ὁ ἀντίδικος ὑμῶν διάβολος ὡς λέων ὠρυόμενος περιπατεῖ ζητῶν τινα καταπιεῖν
verses.transliteration: nēpsate grēgorēsate ho antidikos hymōn diabolos hōs leōn ōryomenos peripatei zētōn tina katapiein
verses.referenceTranslation: Be sober and self-controlled. Be watchful. Your adversary, the devil, walks around like a roaring lion, seeking whom he may devour.
verses.notes: Aorist imperatives νήψατε, γρηγορήσατε. Present participles ὠρυόμενος, ζητῶν. ἀντίδικος — legal adversary.

verses.greek: οὐ γὰρ θελήματι ἀνθρώπου ἠνέχθη προφητεία ποτέ ἀλλ᾽ ὑπὸ πνεύματος ἁγίου φερόμενοι ἐλάλησαν ἀπὸ θεοῦ ἄνθρωποι
verses.transliteration: ou gar thelēmati anthrōpou ēnechthē prophēteia pote all hypo pneumatos hagiou pheromenoi elalēsan apo theou anthrōpoi
verses.referenceTranslation: For no prophecy was ever brought by the will of man, but men spoke from God, being carried along by the Holy Spirit.
verses.notes: Aorist passive ἠνέχθη. Present passive participle φερόμενοι — 'being carried along' by the Spirit.

verses.greek: οὐ βραδύνει κύριος τῆς ἐπαγγελίας ὥς τινες βραδύτητα ἡγοῦνται ἀλλὰ μακροθυμεῖ εἰς ὑμᾶς μὴ βουλόμενός τινας ἀπολέσθαι ἀλλὰ πάντας εἰς μετάνοιαν χωρῆσαι
verses.transliteration: ou bradynei kyrios tēs epangelias hōs tines bradytēta hēgountai alla makrothymei eis hymas mē boulomenos tinas apolesthai alla pantas eis metanoian chōrēsai
verses.referenceTranslation: The Lord is not slow concerning his promise, as some consider slowness, but is patient toward you, not desiring any to perish, but for all to come to repentance.
verses.notes: Present βραδύνει. Present μακροθυμεῖ — 'is patient.' Purpose βουλόμενος — 'desiring all to come to repentance.'

verses.greek: ἐὰν ὁμολογῶμεν τὰς ἁμαρτίας ἡμῶν, πιστός ἐστιν καὶ δίκαιος, ἵνα ἀφῇ ἡμῖν τὰς ἁμαρτίας καὶ καθαρίσῃ ἡμᾶς ἀπὸ πάσης ἀδικίας.
verses.transliteration: Ean homologōmen tas hamartias hēmōn, pistos estin kai dikaios, hina aphē hēmin tas hamartias kai katharisē hēmas apo pasēs adikias.
verses.referenceTranslation: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
verses.notes: Assurance of forgiveness. Third class condition (ἐάν + subjunctive).

verses.greek: ἐὰν ὁμολογῶμεν τὰς ἁμαρτίας ἡμῶν, πιστός ἐστιν καὶ δίκαιος, ἵνα ἀφῇ ἡμῖν τὰς ἁμαρτίας.
verses.transliteration: Ean homologōmen tas hamartias hēmōn, pistos estin kai dikaios, hina aphē hēmin tas hamartias.
verses.referenceTranslation: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins.
verses.notes: Confession and forgiveness. Third class condition (ἐάν + subjunctive).

verses.greek: Τεκνία μου ταῦτα γράφω ὑμῖν ἵνα μὴ ἁμάρτητε καὶ ἐάν τις ἁμάρτῃ παράκλητον ἔχομεν πρὸς τὸν πατέρα Ἰησοῦν Χριστὸν δίκαιον
verses.transliteration: Teknia mou tauta graphō hymin hina mē hamartēte kai ean tis hamartē paraklēton echomen pros ton patera Iēsoun Christon dikaion
verses.referenceTranslation: My little children, I write these things to you so that you may not sin. If anyone sins, we have a Counselor with the Father, Jesus Christ, the righteous.
verses.notes: Purpose clause ἵνα μὴ ἁμάρτητε. Third class condition ἐάν τις ἁμάρτῃ. παράκλητον — 'advocate/counselor.'

verses.greek: καὶ αὐτὸς ἱλασμός ἐστιν περὶ τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν ἡμῶν οὐ περὶ τῶν ἡμετέρων δὲ μόνον ἀλλὰ καὶ περὶ ὅλου τοῦ κόσμου
verses.transliteration: kai autos hilasmos estin peri tōn hamartiōn hēmōn ou peri tōn hēmeterōn de monon alla kai peri holou tou kosmou
verses.referenceTranslation: And he is the propitiation for our sins, and not for ours only but also for the sins of the whole world.
verses.notes: ἱλασμός — 'propitiation/atoning sacrifice.' Universal scope: οὐ...μόνον ἀλλὰ καί — not only ours but the whole world's.

verses.greek: Μὴ ἀγαπᾶτε τὸν κόσμον μηδὲ τὰ ἐν τῷ κόσμῳ ἐάν τις ἀγαπᾷ τὸν κόσμον οὐκ ἔστιν ἡ ἀγάπη τοῦ πατρὸς ἐν αὐτῷ
verses.transliteration: Mē agapate ton kosmon mēde ta en tō kosmō ean tis agapa ton kosmon ouk estin hē agapē tou patros en autō
verses.referenceTranslation: Do not love the world nor the things in the world. If anyone loves the world, the love of the Father is not in him.
verses.notes: Present imperative μὴ ἀγαπᾶτε. Third class condition ἐάν τις ἀγαπᾷ. Incompatibility of world-love and Father-love.

verses.greek: Ἴδετε ποταπὴν ἀγάπην δέδωκεν ἡμῖν ὁ πατήρ ἵνα τέκνα θεοῦ κληθῶμεν καὶ ἐσμέν διὰ τοῦτο ὁ κόσμος οὐ γινώσκει ἡμᾶς ὅτι οὐκ ἔγνω αὐτόν
verses.transliteration: Idete potapēn agapēn dedōken hēmin ho patēr hina tekna theou klēthōmen kai esmen dia touto ho kosmos ou ginōskei hēmas hoti ouk egnō auton
verses.referenceTranslation: Behold what manner of love the Father has given to us, that we should be called children of God — and so we are!
verses.notes: Aorist imperative Ἴδετε. ποταπήν — 'what sort of' (exclamatory). Purpose clause ἵνα κληθῶμεν.

verses.greek: ἐν τούτῳ ἐγνώκαμεν τὴν ἀγάπην ὅτι ἐκεῖνος ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ ἔθηκεν καὶ ἡμεῖς ὀφείλομεν ὑπὲρ τῶν ἀδελφῶν τὰς ψυχὰς θεῖναι
verses.transliteration: en toutō egnōkamen tēn agapēn hoti ekeinos hyper hēmōn tēn psychēn autou ethēken kai hēmeis opheilomen hyper tōn adelphōn tas psychas theinai
verses.referenceTranslation: By this we know love, because he laid down his life for us. And we ought to lay down our lives for the brothers.
verses.notes: Perfect ἐγνώκαμεν. Aorist ἔθηκεν — completed sacrificial act. ὀφείλομεν — moral obligation.

verses.greek: Τεκνία μὴ ἀγαπῶμεν λόγῳ μηδὲ τῇ γλώσσῃ ἀλλ᾽ ἐν ἔργῳ καὶ ἀληθείᾳ
verses.transliteration: Teknia mē agapōmen logō mēde tē glōssē all en ergō kai alētheia
verses.referenceTranslation: Little children, let us not love in word or in tongue, but in action and in truth.
verses.notes: Hortatory subjunctive μὴ ἀγαπῶμεν. Contrast: λόγῳ/γλώσσῃ vs. ἔργῳ/ἀληθείᾳ.

verses.greek: ὁ μὴ ἀγαπῶν οὐκ ἔγνω τὸν θεόν, ὅτι ὁ θεὸς ἀγάπη ἐστίν.
verses.transliteration: Ho mē agapōn ouk egnō ton theon, hoti ho theos agapē estin.
verses.referenceTranslation: Anyone who does not love does not know God, because God is love.
verses.notes: God's essential nature. Note anarthrous ἀγάπη as predicate nominative.

verses.greek: ἐν τούτῳ ἐστὶν ἡ ἀγάπη οὐχ ὅτι ἡμεῖς ἠγαπήκαμεν τὸν θεόν ἀλλ᾽ ὅτι αὐτὸς ἠγάπησεν ἡμᾶς καὶ ἀπέστειλεν τὸν υἱὸν αὐτοῦ ἱλασμὸν περὶ τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν ἡμῶν
verses.transliteration: en toutō estin hē agapē ouch hoti hēmeis ēgapēkamen ton theon all hoti autos ēgapēsen hēmas kai apesteilen ton uion autou hilasmon peri tōn hamartiōn hēmōn
verses.referenceTranslation: In this is love, not that we loved God, but that he loved us, and sent his Son as the atoning sacrifice for our sins.
verses.notes: ἱλασμόν — 'propitiation/atoning sacrifice.' Contrast: οὐχ ὅτι ἡμεῖς...ἀλλ᾽ ὅτι αὐτός.

verses.greek: Ἀγαπητοί εἰ οὕτως ὁ θεὸς ἠγάπησεν ἡμᾶς καὶ ἡμεῖς ὀφείλομεν ἀλλήλους ἀγαπᾶν
verses.transliteration: Agapētoi ei outōs ho theos ēgapēsen hēmas kai hēmeis opheilomen allēlous agapan
verses.referenceTranslation: Beloved, if God so loved us, we also ought to love one another.
verses.notes: Vocative ἀγαπητοί. First class condition εἰ + indicative — assumed true. Present infinitive ἀγαπᾶν.

verses.greek: φόβος οὐκ ἔστιν ἐν τῇ ἀγάπῃ ἀλλ᾽ ἡ τελεία ἀγάπη ἔξω βάλλει τὸν φόβον ὅτι ὁ φόβος κόλασιν ἔχει ὁ δὲ φοβούμενος οὐ τετελείωται ἐν τῇ ἀγάπῃ
verses.transliteration: phobos ouk estin en tē agapē all hē teleia agapē exō ballei ton phobon hoti ho phobos kolasin echei ho de phoboumenos ou teteleiōtai en tē agapē
verses.referenceTranslation: There is no fear in love; but perfect love casts out fear, because fear has punishment. He who fears is not made perfect in love.
verses.notes: Present βάλλει — love keeps casting out fear. Causal ὅτι — fear involves punishment.

verses.greek: ἡμεῖς ἀγαπῶμεν ὅτι αὐτὸς πρῶτος ἠγάπησεν ἡμᾶς
verses.transliteration: hēmeis agapōmen hoti autos prōtos ēgapēsen hēmas
verses.referenceTranslation: We love because He first loved us.
verses.notes: Present ἀγαπῶμεν. Adverb πρῶτος — 'first/before us.' God's prior love is the cause.

verses.greek: αὕτη γάρ ἐστιν ἡ ἀγάπη τοῦ θεοῦ ἵνα τὰς ἐντολὰς αὐτοῦ τηρῶμεν καὶ αἱ ἐντολαὶ αὐτοῦ βαρεῖαι οὐκ εἰσίν
verses.transliteration: autē gar estin hē agapē tou theou hina tas entolas autou tērōmen kai ai entolai autou bareiai ouk eisin
verses.referenceTranslation: For this is loving God, that we keep his commandments. His commandments are not grievous.
verses.notes: ἀγάπη τοῦ θεοῦ — subjective or objective genitive. Purpose clause ἵνα τηρῶμεν. βαρεῖαι — 'heavy/burdensome.'

verses.greek: καὶ αὕτη ἐστὶν ἡ μαρτυρία ὅτι ζωὴν αἰώνιον ἔδωκεν ἡμῖν ὁ θεός καὶ αὕτη ἡ ζωὴ ἐν τῷ υἱῷ αὐτοῦ ἐστιν
verses.transliteration: kai autē estin hē martyria hoti zōēn aiōnion edōken hēmin ho theos kai autē hē zōē en tō uiō autou estin
verses.referenceTranslation: The testimony is this, that God gave to us eternal life, and this life is in his Son.
verses.notes: Demonstrative αὕτη. Aorist ἔδωκεν. ἐν τῷ υἱῷ — life is located in the Son.

verses.greek: ὁ ἔχων τὸν υἱὸν ἔχει τὴν ζωήν ὁ μὴ ἔχων τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ θεοῦ τὴν ζωὴν οὐκ ἔχει
verses.transliteration: ho echōn ton uion echei tēn zōēn ho mē echōn ton uion tou theou tēn zōēn ouk echei
verses.referenceTranslation: He who has the Son has the life. He who doesn’t have God’s Son doesn’t have the life.
verses.notes: Parallel structure: ὁ ἔχων/ὁ μὴ ἔχων. Present ἔχει — current possession. Absolute: no middle ground.

verses.greek: Ταῦτα ἔγραψα ὑμῖν ἵνα εἰδῆτε ὅτι ζωὴν ἔχετε αἰώνιον τοῖς πιστεύουσιν εἰς τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ υἱοῦ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.transliteration: Tauta egrapsa hymin hina eidēte hoti zōēn echete aiōnion tois pisteuousin eis to onoma tou uiou tou theou
verses.referenceTranslation: These things I have written to you who believe in the name of the Son of God, that you may know that you have eternal life, and that you may continue to believe in the name of the Son of God.
verses.notes: Aorist ἔγραψα — epistolary aorist. Purpose clause ἵνα εἰδῆτε. Perfect εἰδῆτε — settled knowledge.

verses.greek: Τῷ δὲ δυναμένῳ φυλάξαι ὑμᾶς ἀπταίστους καὶ στῆσαι κατενώπιον τῆς δόξης αὐτοῦ ἀμώμους ἐν ἀγαλλιάσει
verses.transliteration: Tō de dynamenō phylaxai hymas aptaistous kai stēsai katenōpion tēs doxēs autou amōmous en agalliasei
verses.referenceTranslation: Now to him who is able to keep them from stumbling, and to present you faultless before the presence of his glory in great joy,
verses.notes: Doxology begins. Aorist infinitives φυλάξαι, στῆσαι. ἀπταίστους — 'without stumbling.'

verses.greek: μόνῳ θεῷ σωτῆρι ἡμῶν διὰ Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν δόξα μεγαλωσύνη κράτος καὶ ἐξουσία πρὸ παντὸς τοῦ αἰῶνος καὶ νῦν καὶ εἰς πάντας τοὺς αἰῶνας ἀμήν
verses.transliteration: monō theō sōtēri hēmōn dia Iēsou Christou tou kyriou hēmōn doxa megalōsynē kratos kai exousia pro pantos tou aiōnos kai nyn kai eis pantas tous aiōnas amēn
verses.referenceTranslation: to the only God our Savior, through Jesus Christ our Lord, be glory, majesty, dominion, and authority, before all time, now, and forever. Amen.
verses.notes: Four-fold ascription: δόξα, μεγαλωσύνη, κράτος, ἐξουσία. Three temporal markers: πρὸ παντὸς τοῦ αἰῶνος, νῦν, εἰς πάντας τοὺς αἰῶνας.

verses.greek: Ἐγώ εἰμι τὸ ἄλφα καὶ τὸ ὦ, λέγει κύριος ὁ θεός, ὁ ὢν καὶ ὁ ἦν καὶ ὁ ἐρχόμενος, ὁ παντοκράτωρ.
verses.transliteration: Egō eimi to alpha kai to ō, legei kyrios ho theos, ho ōn kai ho ēn kai ho erchomenos, ho pantokratōr.
verses.referenceTranslation: 'I am the Alpha and the Omega,' says the Lord God, 'who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty.'
verses.notes: Divine self-declaration. Unusual grammar with periphrastic ὁ ἦν.

verses.greek: Ἰδοὺ ἕστηκα ἐπὶ τὴν θύραν καὶ κρούω ἐάν τις ἀκούσῃ τῆς φωνῆς μου καὶ ἀνοίξῃ τὴν θύραν καὶ εἰσελεύσομαι πρὸς αὐτὸν καὶ δειπνήσω μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ καὶ αὐτὸς μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ
verses.transliteration: Idou hestēka epi tēn thyran kai krouō ean tis akousē tēs phōnēs mou kai anoixē tēn thyran kai eiseleusomai pros auton kai deipnēsō met autou kai autos met emou
verses.referenceTranslation: Behold, I stand at the door and knock. If anyone hears my voice and opens the door, I will come in to him and will dine with him, and he with me.
verses.notes: Perfect ἕστηκα — 'I stand (and remain).' Present κρούω. Third class condition ἐάν τις.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐξαλείψει πᾶν δάκρυον ἐκ τῶν ὀφθαλμῶν αὐτῶν, καὶ ὁ θάνατος οὐκ ἔσται ἔτι· οὔτε πένθος οὔτε κραυγὴ οὔτε πόνος οὐκ ἔσται ἔτι, ὅτι τὰ πρῶτα ἀπῆλθαν.
verses.transliteration: Kai exaleipsei pan dakryon ek tōn ophthalmōn autōn, kai ho thanatos ouk estai eti; oute penthos oute kraugē oute ponos ouk estai eti, hoti ta prōta apēlthan.
verses.referenceTranslation: He will wipe away every tear from their eyes, and death shall be no more, neither shall there be mourning, nor crying, nor pain anymore, for the former things have passed away.
verses.notes: New creation promise. Allusion to Isaiah 25:8.

verses.greek: ἐγὼ τὸ ἄλφα καὶ τὸ ὦ ὁ πρῶτος καὶ ὁ ἔσχατος ἡ ἀρχὴ καὶ τὸ τέλος
verses.transliteration: egō to alpha kai to ō ho prōtos kai ho eschatos hē archē kai to telos
verses.referenceTranslation: I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last, the Beginning and the End.”
verses.notes: Three pairs of merisms expressing totality. ἀρχή/τέλος — temporal completeness.

verses.greek: Καὶ τὸ πνεῦμα καὶ ἡ νύμφη λέγουσιν ἔρχου καὶ ὁ ἀκούων εἰπάτω ἔρχου καὶ ὁ διψῶν ἐρχέσθω ὁ θέλων λαβέτω ὕδωρ ζωῆς δωρεάν
verses.transliteration: Kai to pneuma kai hē nymphē legousin erchou kai ho akouōn eipatō erchou kai ho dipsōn erchesthō ho thelōn labetō hydōr zōēs dōrean
verses.referenceTranslation: The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” He who hears, let him say, “Come!” He who is thirsty, let him come. He who desires, let him take the water of life freely.
verses.notes: Three invitations to 'come.' δωρεάν — 'as a gift/freely.' Present participle ὁ διψῶν.

verses.greek: Λέγει ὁ μαρτυρῶν ταῦτα ναί ἔρχομαι ταχύ Ἀμήν ἔρχου κύριε Ἰησοῦ
verses.transliteration: Legei ho martyrōn tauta nai erchomai tachy Amēn erchou kyrie Iēsou
verses.referenceTranslation: He who testifies these things says, "Yes, I am coming quickly." Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!
verses.notes: Present middle μαρτυρῶν. ναί — affirmation. ταχύ — 'soon/quickly.' Maranatha echo.

verses.greek: Ἡ χάρις τοῦ κυρίου Ἰησοῦ μετὰ πάντων
verses.transliteration: Hē charis tou kyriou Iēsou meta pantōn
verses.referenceTranslation: The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all the saints. Amen.
verses.notes: Benediction closing the NT. μετὰ πάντων — 'with all the saints.'

verses.greek: Παῦλος δέσμιος Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ καὶ Τιμόθεος ὁ ἀδελφὸς Φιλήμονι τῷ ἀγαπητῷ καὶ συνεργῷ ἡμῶν
verses.referenceTranslation: Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, to Philemon our beloved and fellow worker,
verses.notes: δέσμιος — 'prisoner,' not δοῦλος. συνεργῷ — 'co-worker.' Dative Φιλήμονι — recipient.
verses.transliteration: Paulos desmios Christou Iēsou kai Timotheos ho adelphos Philēmoni tō agapētō kai synergō hēmōn

verses.greek: ὅπως ἡ κοινωνία τῆς πίστεώς σου ἐνεργὴς γένηται ἐν ἐπιγνώσει παντὸς ἀγαθοῦ τοῦ ἐν ἡμῖν εἰς Χριστόν
verses.referenceTranslation: that the fellowship of your faith may become effective through the knowledge of every good thing that is in us for Christ.
verses.notes: ὅπως + subjunctive γένηται — purpose clause. ἐνεργής — 'effective/active.' ἐπίγνωσις — 'full knowledge.'
verses.transliteration: hopōs hē koinōnia tēs pisteōs sou energēs genētai en epignōsei pantos agathou tou en hēmin eis Christon

verses.greek: τάχα γὰρ διὰ τοῦτο ἐχωρίσθη πρὸς ὥραν ἵνα αἰώνιον αὐτὸν ἀπέχῃς
verses.referenceTranslation: For perhaps he was separated from you for a time so that you might have him back forever,
verses.notes: τάχα — 'perhaps,' diplomatic understatement. Aorist passive ἐχωρίσθη — divine passive. πρὸς ὥραν vs. αἰώνιον contrast.
verses.transliteration: tacha gar dia touto echōristhē pros hōran hina aiōnion auton apechēs

verses.greek: οὐκέτι ὡς δοῦλον ἀλλ᾽ ὑπὲρ δοῦλον ἀδελφὸν ἀγαπητόν μάλιστα ἐμοί πόσῳ δὲ μᾶλλον σοὶ καὶ ἐν σαρκὶ καὶ ἐν κυρίῳ
verses.referenceTranslation: no longer as a slave, but more than a slave, a beloved brother — especially to me, but how much more to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.
verses.notes: οὐκέτι...ἀλλ᾽ ὑπέρ — 'no longer...but beyond.' Dual sphere: ἐν σαρκί (earthly) and ἐν κυρίῳ (spiritual).
verses.transliteration: ouketi hōs doulon all hyper doulon adelphon agapēton malista emoi posō de mallon soi kai en sarki kai en kyriō

verses.greek: Ὁ πρεσβύτερος ἐκλεκτῇ κυρίᾳ καὶ τοῖς τέκνοις αὐτῆς οὓς ἐγὼ ἀγαπῶ ἐν ἀληθείᾳ
verses.referenceTranslation: The elder to the elect lady and her children, whom I love in truth,
verses.notes: πρεσβύτερος — 'elder,' self-designation. ἐκλεκτῇ κυρίᾳ — debated: personal name or a church personified.
verses.transliteration: Ho presbyteros eklektē kyria kai tois teknois autēs ous egō agapō en alētheia

verses.greek: Ἐχάρην λίαν ὅτι εὕρηκα ἐκ τῶν τέκνων σου περιπατοῦντας ἐν ἀληθείᾳ καθὼς ἐντολὴν ἐλάβομεν παρὰ τοῦ πατρός
verses.referenceTranslation: I rejoiced greatly that I have found some of your children walking in truth, just as we received commandment from the Father.
verses.notes: Aorist passive ἐχάρην — ingressive. Perfect εὕρηκα — 'I have found.' ἐκ partitive — 'some of.'
verses.transliteration: Echarēn lian hoti eurēka ek tōn teknōn sou peripatountas en alētheia kathōs entolēn elabomen para tou patros

verses.greek: καὶ αὕτη ἐστὶν ἡ ἀγάπη ἵνα περιπατῶμεν κατὰ τὰς ἐντολὰς αὐτοῦ αὕτη ἡ ἐντολή ἐστιν καθὼς ἠκούσατε ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς ἵνα ἐν αὐτῇ περιπατῆτε
verses.referenceTranslation: And this is love, that we walk according to his commandments. This is the commandment, just as you heard from the beginning, that you should walk in it.
verses.notes: Two ἵνα clauses define love and the commandment. ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς — 'from the beginning' of Christian instruction.
verses.transliteration: kai autē estin hē agapē hina peripatōmen kata tas entolas autou autē hē entolē estin kathōs ēkousate ap archēs hina en autē peripatēte

verses.greek: Ὁ πρεσβύτερος Γαΐῳ τῷ ἀγαπητῷ ὃν ἐγὼ ἀγαπῶ ἐν ἀληθείᾳ
verses.referenceTranslation: The elder to the beloved Gaius, whom I love in truth.
verses.notes: Same self-designation πρεσβύτερος as 2 John. Dative Γαΐῳ — named recipient. ἐν ἀληθείᾳ — 'in truth.'
verses.transliteration: Ho presbyteros Gaiō tō agapētō hon egō agapō en alētheia

verses.greek: μειζοτέραν τούτων οὐκ ἔχω χαράν ἵνα ἀκούω τὰ ἐμὰ τέκνα ἐν τῇ ἀληθείᾳ περιπατοῦντα
verses.referenceTranslation: I have no greater joy than this, to hear that my children are walking in the truth.
verses.notes: Double comparative μειζοτέραν (from μέγας). Epexegetical ἵνα clause. Present participle περιπατοῦντα.
verses.transliteration: meizoteran toutōn ouk echō charan hina akouō ta ema tekna en tē alētheia peripatounta

verses.greek: Ἀγαπητέ μὴ μιμοῦ τὸ κακὸν ἀλλὰ τὸ ἀγαθόν ὁ ἀγαθοποιῶν ἐκ τοῦ θεοῦ ἐστιν ὁ κακοποιῶν οὐχ ἑώρακεν τὸν θεόν
verses.referenceTranslation: Beloved, do not imitate what is evil, but what is good. The one who does good is of God; the one who does evil has not seen God.
verses.notes: Vocative ἀγαπητέ. Present imperative μὴ μιμοῦ. Substantival participles ὁ ἀγαθοποιῶν, ὁ κακοποιῶν. Perfect ἑώρακεν.
verses.transliteration: Agapēte mē mimou to kakon alla to agathon ho agathopoiōn ek tou theou estin ho kakopoiōn ouch heōraken ton theon
